## @sganesan_dumac

Do both

## Copilot

Yes — here are both, grounded in the actual script structure you shared.

## First: key implementation observations from the canonical script

Your script materially changes the plan in a few ways:

- **Section ordering is inconsistent.** The file starts with a holdings-processing block labeled “SECTION 2” before the later “SECTION 1” block, and then defines Section 2 again later. So a wrapper that simply `source()`s the file will execute more than you want unless you modify the script.  
- `company_map` is used before it is built in the top-of-file holdings call at lines 143–146, while `company_map <- build_company_resolution()` happens later at lines 189–191. That means the current file order is not safe for clean review-mode execution.
- The script really does produce natural checkpoint candidates:
  - `company_map`, `rd_history`, `current_holdings`, `daily_ratios`, `patent_data`, `master_snapshot` in Section 1
  - `holdings_lookup` in Section 2
  - `master_tickers` in Section 3
- The repo already has a sensible structure under `R/01_utils`, `R/02_ingest`, `R/03_transform`, etc., so adding a small helper under `R/01_utils` is cleaner than stuffing all review-support helpers into the canonical script.

That leads to one strong recommendation:

## Recommendation
Do **not** try to make review-mode work by only adding a wrapper.  
You need **small conditional logic in the main script** plus a **small helper file** plus a **wrapper**.

---

# 1) Implementation-ready pseudo-diff plan

This is not exact code, but a concrete modification plan you or a developer can implement safely.

---

## A. Add a review-support helper file

**New file**
`R/01_utils/review_support.R`

### Add functions
- `is_review_mode()`
- `get_stop_after_section()`
- `setup_review_output_dir()`
- `save_review_checkpoint()`
- `write_validation_summary()`
- `review_log()`
- maybe `stop_if_review_section_complete(section_num)`

### Purpose
Keep reviewer-support logic out of the main business logic as much as possible.

### Pseudo-diff
```r
# R/01_utils/review_support.R

is_review_mode <- function() {
  identical(tolower(Sys.getenv("RUN_REVIEW_MODE", "false")), "true")
}

get_stop_after_section <- function() {
  Sys.getenv("STOP_AFTER_SECTION", "")
}

setup_review_output_dir <- function() {
  run_timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
  base_dir <- here::here("outputs", "section_checkpoints", paste0("run_", run_timestamp))
  dir.create(base_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  base_dir
}

review_log <- function(...) {
  cat("[REVIEW MODE]", ..., "\n")
}

save_review_checkpoint <- function(obj, path, format = c("csv", "rds")) {
  format <- match.arg(format)
  dir.create(dirname(path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  if (format == "csv") write.csv(obj, path, row.names = FALSE)
  if (format == "rds") saveRDS(obj, path)
}

write_validation_summary <- function(obj, file_path, obj_name, critical_cols = NULL, unique_key = NULL) {
  lines <- c(
    paste("Validation summary for:", obj_name),
    paste("Timestamp:", Sys.time()),
    paste("Rows:", if (!is.null(nrow(obj))) nrow(obj) else NA_integer_),
    paste("Columns:", paste(colnames(obj), collapse = ", "))
  )

  if (!is.null(critical_cols)) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Missing critical columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    for (col in intersect(critical_cols, colnames(obj))) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("NA count for", col, ":", sum(is.na(obj[[col]]))))
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(unique_key) && unique_key %in% colnames(obj)) {
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Duplicate", unique_key, "count:", sum(duplicated(obj[[unique_key]]))))
  }

  writeLines(lines, con = file_path)
}

stop_if_review_section_complete <- function(section_num) {
  stop_after <- get_stop_after_section()
  if (is_review_mode() && nzchar(stop_after) && identical(as.character(section_num), stop_after)) {
    review_log("Stopping after section", section_num)
    stop(structure(list(message = paste("REVIEW_STOP_SECTION", section_num)), class = c("review_stop", "condition")))
  }
}
```

---

## B. Load the helper early in the canonical script

Near the top of the script, before Section 1 logic:

### Pseudo-diff
```r
library(here)

source(here("R", "01_utils", "review_support.R"))

review_mode <- is_review_mode()
review_output_dir <- NULL

if (review_mode) {
  review_output_dir <- setup_review_output_dir()
  review_log("Reviewer output directory:", review_output_dir)
}
```

---

## C. Remove or guard the premature top-of-file Section 2 holdings execution

This is one of the biggest issues in the script.

At the top of the file, before Section 1, the script defines and executes holdings logic using `company_map` before it exists.

### Recommended approach
Wrap that entire early Section 2 block in a guard so it does **not** run in review mode, or ideally remove it if it is duplicated by the later Section 2 block.

### Pseudo-diff
```r
# Guard legacy/preliminary holdings block
if (!is_review_mode()) {
  # existing early holdings block at top of file
}
```

### Better long-term fix
Delete the duplicate top block and keep only the later canonical Section 2 block.  
But if you want low risk, guard it first rather than refactoring it out tomorrow morning.

---

## D. Add N=25 sample filtering support in review mode

The wrapper should define sample tickers, but the canonical script needs to **use** them.

### Add a helper pattern
```r
review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if (review_mode && exists("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- get("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)
}
```

### Then after `company_map` is built
```r
if (review_mode && !is.null(review_sample_tickers)) {
  company_map <- company_map %>% filter(Ticker %in% review_sample_tickers | ticker %in% review_sample_tickers)
  review_log("Filtered company_map to N=25 sample. Rows:", nrow(company_map))
}
```

You may need to normalize `Ticker` vs `ticker`.

---

## E. Add Section 1 checkpoint saves

After each major object creation in Section 1:

### After `company_map <- build_company_resolution()`
```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  save_review_checkpoint(company_map, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_company_map.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    company_map,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_company_map_validation.txt"),
    "company_map",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker", "ISIN"),
    unique_key = "Ticker"
  )
}
```

### After `rd_history <- pull_rd_history(company_map)`
```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  rd_sample <- head(rd_history, 100)
  save_review_checkpoint(rd_sample, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_rd_history_sample.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    rd_history,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_rd_history_validation.txt"),
    "rd_history",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker", "revenue"),
    unique_key = NULL
  )
}
```

### After `master_snapshot` is fully created and deduped
```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  save_review_checkpoint(master_snapshot, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot.rds"), "rds")
  save_review_checkpoint(master_snapshot, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    master_snapshot,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot_validation.txt"),
    "master_snapshot",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker"),
    unique_key = "Ticker"
  )
}
```

### Then stop if needed
```r
stop_if_review_section_complete(1)
```

---

## F. Add Section 2 checkpoint saves

After `holdings_lookup <- process_holdings_data_fixed(...)` in the later Section 2 block:

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section2_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section2")
  save_review_checkpoint(holdings_lookup, file.path(section2_dir, "section2_holdings_lookup.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    holdings_lookup,
    file.path(section2_dir, "section2_holdings_lookup_validation.txt"),
    "holdings_lookup",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker", "matched_by", "in_portfolio"),
    unique_key = "Ticker"
  )
}
stop_if_review_section_complete(2)
```

---

## G. Add Section 3 checkpoint saves

After `master_tickers <- unique(c(rd_history$ticker, holdings_lookup$Ticker))`

Prefer saving as a simple data frame:

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section3_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section3")
  master_tickers_df <- data.frame(ticker = master_tickers, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  save_review_checkpoint(master_tickers_df, file.path(section3_dir, "section3_master_ticker_list.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    master_tickers_df,
    file.path(section3_dir, "section3_master_ticker_list_validation.txt"),
    "master_ticker_list",
    critical_cols = c("ticker"),
    unique_key = "ticker"
  )
}
stop_if_review_section_complete(3)
```

---

## H. Build one wrapper script

**New file**
`run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

### Pseudo-diff
```r
library(here)
library(utils)

Sys.setenv(RUN_REVIEW_MODE = "true")
Sys.setenv(STOP_AFTER_SECTION = "3")

n25_path <- here::here("data", "00_reference", "n25_representative_sample.csv")
n25_df <- read.csv(n25_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

review_sample_tickers <- unique(if ("ticker" %in% names(n25_df)) n25_df$ticker else n25_df$Ticker)
assign("review_sample_tickers", review_sample_tickers, envir = .GlobalEnv)

source(here::here("DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r"))
```

### Important
Because `stop_if_review_section_complete()` raises a condition, you may want to wrap `source()` in `tryCatch()` and treat `"review_stop"` as success.

```r
tryCatch(
  source(here::here("DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r")),
  review_stop = function(e) message("Review-mode stop reached as expected: ", e$message)
)
```

---

## I. Update PR run instructions after pilot

Once the wrapper works, update PR comments to say reviewers should run:

```r
source("run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R")
```

or from command line via `Rscript`.

# 2) Developer issue / task ticket

Here is a clean issue you can use internally.

````markdown
# Add reviewer-support execution mode for Sprint 1 Sections 1–3

## Summary
Implement lightweight reviewer-support changes so Sprint 1 Sections 1–3 can be executed on the N=25 sample with checkpoint outputs and validation summaries, without changing underlying business logic.

## Background
Current Sprint 1 review is organized into section-level PRs, but the canonical script is a monolithic integrated pipeline. Reviewers need a reliable way to:
- run the Sprint 1 scope on the N=25 sample
- inspect section-level outputs
- validate key intermediate objects
- document decomposition findings in PRs

The current script structure makes this difficult because:
- early holdings logic appears before company resolution is built
- Section 2 logic is duplicated
- key outputs are mostly in-memory objects
- plain `source()` will run the full file unless review-mode stopping logic is added

## Goal
Add a review execution mode for Sprint 1 Sections 1–3 that:
- runs on the N=25 sample
- saves reviewer-friendly checkpoint outputs
- writes validation summaries
- stops cleanly after Section 1, 2, or 3 as configured
- does not alter underlying section business logic

## Deliverables

### 1. Review-support helper file
Create:
- `R/01_utils/review_support.R`

Include helper functions for:
- review-mode detection
- reviewer output directory setup
- checkpoint saving
- validation summary writing
- section-stop handling

### 2. Canonical script review-mode support
Update:
- `DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r`

Add:
- helper sourcing
- review-mode initialization
- N=25 sample filtering support
- checkpoint saves after Sections 1–3
- clean stop-after-section logic

Also guard or remove the premature top-of-file holdings execution block so review-mode execution is consistent.

### 3. Reviewer wrapper script
Create:
- `run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

This wrapper should:
- enable review mode
- load the N=25 sample
- expose sample tickers to the main script
- source the canonical script
- stop after Section 3
- print success/output-location messaging

### 4. Reviewer checkpoint outputs
Implement checkpoint outputs for:
- Section 1:
  - company mapping object
  - historical R&D sample/object summary
  - integrated snapshot object
- Section 2:
  - holdings lookup output
- Section 3:
  - master ticker list

### 5. Validation summaries
Add reviewer-readable validation summaries including:
- row counts
- schema/column presence
- missingness for critical fields
- unique-key / duplicate-key checks
- file existence checks
- warnings/errors where relevant

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Review mode can be enabled with environment variables or equivalent configuration
- [ ] Wrapper runs the canonical script through Section 3 on the N=25 sample
- [ ] Section 1 checkpoint files are written successfully
- [ ] Section 2 checkpoint files are written successfully
- [ ] Section 3 checkpoint files are written successfully
- [ ] Validation summary files are created for each section
- [ ] Review-mode execution does not continue into downstream sections when stop-after-section is set
- [ ] Changes do not alter underlying business logic or downstream production behavior
- [ ] Pilot run is completed and outputs are reviewed before reviewer release

## Notes
This work is intended to improve reviewer observability and validation only. It should not expand PR approval scope or change governance boundaries for Sprint 1 section reviews.
````

---

## Final recommendation

### Do first
1. Implement the helper file
2. Guard the premature duplicate Section 2 block
3. Add review-mode checkpoints and stop logic
4. Build the wrapper
5. Pilot with Section 1/through Section 3 on N=25

### Do after pilot
6. Update PR comments with the real run instructions and real output file names

If you want, next I can draft the **actual code skeletons** for:
- `R/01_utils/review_support.R`
- `run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`



## @sganesan_dumac

Do you have access to the canonical script? I am going to give you a raw git link to the pipeline script in my public repository for this project which is identical to what is in my enterprise repository. Is this plan feasible?

## Copilot

Yes — I do have access to the canonical script content you pasted earlier, and I also reviewed the repository file via GitHub tools in your enterprise repo.

## Short answer

**Yes, the plan is feasible.**  
But it is feasible as a **lightweight review-support adaptation**, not as a no-code wrapper-only solution.

### What is feasible
- adding review-mode helpers
- adding checkpoint outputs after Sections 1–3
- adding validation summaries
- adding a wrapper script for N=25 reviewer runs
- stopping execution after Section 3

### What is not feasible without code edits
- using a wrapper alone to “run only Sections 1–3” if the canonical script continues through the full pipeline
- assuming Sections 1–3 are independently runnable without handling shared objects and current script ordering issues

## Why I’m confident it’s feasible

Because the current script already has identifiable section boundaries and named intermediate objects:

- Section 1 creates objects like `company_map`, `rd_history`, `current_holdings`, `daily_ratios`, `patent_data`, `master_snapshot`
- Section 2 creates `holdings_lookup`
- Section 3 creates `master_tickers`

Those are exactly the kinds of natural checkpoint points you need.

## Main caveat

The current file also has structural issues that mean the plan must be implemented carefully:

- a holdings block appears before the later Section 1 build of `company_map`
- Section 2 logic appears duplicated
- the file mixes setup, execution, and output-generation concerns
- downstream sections assume shared global objects

So the plan is feasible, but only if you add **review-mode guards** and **checkpoint logic** inside the canonical script.

Yes — send it.

Once you share the raw public link, I can:
1. confirm whether it matches the version we already inspected,
2. sanity-check whether the implementation plan fits that exact file,
3. refine the pseudo-diff plan against the public copy.

## Bottom line

**Yes, the plan is feasible.**  
**No, it should not be attempted as wrapper-only.**  
It should be implemented as:
- a helper file,
- small guarded changes in the canonical script,
- and one review wrapper for Sections 1–3.

# Proposed coding sequence

## Code Item 1
Add a **review-support block** near the top of the pipeline script:
- environment flags
- output directory helpers
- logging helpers
- validation writer
- stop helper

## Code Item 2
Guard the **early duplicate holdings block** so it does not run in review mode.

Add **Section 1 checkpoint saves and validation summaries**.

## Code Item 4
Add **Section 2 checkpoint saves and validation summaries**.

## Code Item 5
Add **Section 3 checkpoint saves and validation summaries**.

## Code Item 6
Add **review-mode stop logic** after Sections 1, 2, and 3.

## Code Item 7
Add the **review wrapper script**.

---

# How I recommend we do this in chat

We should do **one code item at a time**.

For each one, I’ll give you:
1. **what to replace or insert**
2. **the complete code block**
3. **where it goes**
4. **what to test next**

That will be much safer than dumping everything at once.

# Start with Code Item 1

This first item adds the helper functions directly into the pipeline script so you can move fast without creating a separate helper file yet.

## Insert this near the top of the script, before the first executable section logic

Use this as a new block near the top of the file, ideally right after the banner comments and before the current early holdings block.

```r
# =============================================================================
# REVIEW SUPPORT MODE
# =============================================================================
# This block adds lightweight reviewer-support functionality for Sprint 1
# Sections 1-3. It is intended to improve observability and validation only.
# It should not change underlying business logic when review mode is off.

RUN_REVIEW_MODE <- identical(tolower(Sys.getenv("RUN_REVIEW_MODE", "false")), "true")
STOP_AFTER_SECTION <- Sys.getenv("STOP_AFTER_SECTION", "")

review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if (RUN_REVIEW_MODE && exists("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- get("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

review_log <- function(...) {
  cat("[REVIEW MODE]", ..., "\n")
}

setup_review_output_dir <- function() {
  run_timestamp_review <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
  base_dir <- here::here("outputs", "section_checkpoints", paste0("run_", run_timestamp_review))
  dir.create(base_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  return(base_dir)
}

save_review_checkpoint <- function(obj, file_path, format = c("csv", "rds")) {
  format <- match.arg(format)
  dir.create(dirname(file_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  if (format == "csv") {
    write.csv(obj, file_path, row.names = FALSE)
  } else if (format == "rds") {
    saveRDS(obj, file_path)
  }
}

write_validation_summary <- function(obj, file_path, obj_name,
                                     critical_cols = NULL,
                                     unique_key = NULL) {
  lines <- c(
    paste("Validation summary for:", obj_name),
    paste("Timestamp:", Sys.time()),
    paste("Rows:", if (!is.null(nrow(obj))) nrow(obj) else NA),
    paste("Columns:", paste(colnames(obj), collapse = ", "))
  )

  if (!is.null(critical_cols)) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("Missing critical columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    } else {
      lines <- c(lines, "Missing critical columns: none")
    }

    present_cols <- intersect(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    for (col in present_cols) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("NA count for", col, ":", sum(is.na(obj[[col]]))))
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(unique_key) && unique_key %in% colnames(obj)) {
    dup_count <- sum(duplicated(obj[[unique_key]]))
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Duplicate", unique_key, "count:", dup_count))
  }

  writeLines(lines, con = file_path)
}

stop_if_review_section_complete <- function(section_num) {
  if (RUN_REVIEW_MODE &&
      nzchar(STOP_AFTER_SECTION) &&
      identical(as.character(section_num), STOP_AFTER_SECTION)) {
    review_log(paste("Stopping after section", section_num))
    stop(paste("REVIEW_STOP_SECTION", section_num))
  }
}

review_output_dir <- NULL
if (RUN_REVIEW_MODE) {
  if (!requireNamespace("here", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'here' is required for review mode.")
  }
  review_output_dir <- setup_review_output_dir()
  review_log(paste("Reviewer output directory:", review_output_dir))
}
```

---

# What this does

This gives you:

- `RUN_REVIEW_MODE`
- `STOP_AFTER_SECTION`
- `review_sample_tickers`
- output folder creation
- checkpoint saving
- validation summary writing
- stop-after-section helper

This is the foundation for everything else.

```
################################################################################
# DAII 3.5 - COMPLETE INTEGRATED PIPELINE - FINAL FIXED VERSION
# Version: 5.3 FINAL | Date: 2026-03-18 (with post‑review corrections)
#
# SECTION INDEX:
#   1  DATA INTEGRATION (live data sources)
#   2  LOAD HOLDINGS DATA (DUMAC portfolio)
#   3  CREATE MASTER TICKER LIST
#   4  LOAD PRE‑FETCHED PANEL DATA
#   5  CREATE COMPANY SNAPSHOT (one row per ticker)
#   6  PROCEED TO MAIN PIPELINE (load snapshot, ensure columns)
#   7  INNOVATION SCORING
#   8  MERGE HOLDINGS DATA (add portfolio flags)
#   9  AI INTENSITY SCORING & PORTFOLIO CONSTRUCTION
#  10  AI CUBE & ANOMALY DETECTION
#  11  AGGRESSIVE AI ANALYSIS (UNICORN HUNTING)
#  12  BENCHMARK CONSTITUENTS & COMPARISON
#  13  BENCHMARK AI EXPOSURE ANALYSIS
#  14  CONTROL BENCHMARKS (AI‑Low, Matched‑Pair)
#  15  PERFORMANCE ATTRIBUTION
#  16  UNIVERSAL CONTROL PANEL (OPTIONAL)
#  17  OUTPUT GENERATION
################################################################################

# Load required packages
library(dplyr)
library(magrittr)
library(DBI)
library(odbc)
library(here)

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 1: DATA INTEGRATION (LIVE DATA SOURCES)
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📊 SECTION 1: INTEGRATING LIVE DATA SOURCES\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.1 Source all ingestion scripts
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
source(here("R", "01_utils", "database_connection.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "01_company_resolution.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "02_pull_rd_history.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "03_pull_holdings.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "04_pull_daily_ratios.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "05_patentsview.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "06_pull_bloomberg_api.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "07_load_bloomberg_cache.r"))
source(here("R", "02_ingest", "09_pull_msci.r"))
source(here("R", "03_transform", "01_create_snapshot.r"))
source(here("R", "03_transform", "02_calculate_features.r"))
source(here("R", "03_transform", "03_handle_missing.r"))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.2 Build company resolution table (MUST RUN FIRST)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("🔍 Building company resolution table...\n")
company_map <- build_company_resolution()
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Mapped %d companies\n", nrow(company_map)))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.3 Pull 40-year R&D history from Refinitiv
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n📈 Pulling 40-year R&D history...\n")
rd_history <- pull_rd_history(company_map)
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Pulled %d rows of historical R&D data\n", nrow(rd_history)))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.4 Pull current holdings from risk_exposure
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🏦 Pulling current holdings...\n")
current_holdings <- pull_current_holdings()
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Pulled %d holdings records\n", nrow(current_holdings)))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.5 Pull daily ratios from Refinitiv
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n📊 Pulling daily ratios...\n")
daily_ratios <- pull_daily_ratios(company_map, start_date = "2024-01-01")
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Pulled %d daily ratio records\n", nrow(daily_ratios)))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.6 Pull patent data from USPTO bulk files (with automatic quarterly update)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🔬 Pulling AI patent data from USPTO bulk files...\n")
BULK_DIR <- here::here("data", "01_raw", "uspto_bulk/")
ASSIGNEE_FILE <- file.path(BULK_DIR, "g_assignee_disambiguated.tsv")
CPC_FILE <- file.path(BULK_DIR, "g_cpc_current.tsv")

need_update <- FALSE
if(!file.exists(ASSIGNEE_FILE) || !file.exists(CPC_FILE)) {
  need_update <- TRUE
  cat("   Bulk files missing. Will download.\n")
} else {
  file_age <- difftime(Sys.time(), file.info(ASSIGNEE_FILE)$mtime, units = "days")
  if(file_age > 90) {
    need_update <- TRUE
    cat(sprintf("   Bulk files are %.0f days old. Updating...\n", file_age))
  } else {
    cat(sprintf("   ✅ Bulk files are %.0f days old\n", file_age))
  }
}
if(need_update) {
  source("R/02_ingest/06_update_uspto_bulk.r")
  update_uspto_bulk()
}

patent_data <- pull_patent_data(company_map, use_cache = TRUE)
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Pulled patent data for %d companies\n", nrow(patent_data)))
patent_data$patent_source <- "USPTO Only"

european_countries <- c("DEU", "GBR", "FRA", "NLD", "CHE", "SWE", "FIN", "ITA", "ESP", "AUT", "BEL", "DNK", "NOR", "IRL")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.6.1 Pull European patent data
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🇪🇺 Pulling European patent data...\n")
source("R/02_ingest/12_epo_api.r")
european_patents <- pull_european_patents(company_map, use_cache = TRUE)
if(nrow(european_patents) > 0) {
  if("Ticker" %in% names(patent_data) && !"ticker" %in% names(patent_data)) {
    patent_data$ticker <- patent_data$Ticker
  }
  if(all(c("ticker", "total_patents", "ai_patents") %in% names(european_patents))) {
    patent_data <- patent_data %>%
      left_join(european_patents %>% select(ticker, total_patents, ai_patents), 
                by = "ticker", suffix = c("", "_euro")) %>%
      mutate(
        total_patents = ifelse(is.na(total_patents_euro), total_patents, total_patents + total_patents_euro),
        ai_patents = ifelse(is.na(ai_patents_euro), ai_patents, ai_patents + ai_patents_euro),
        patent_source = case_when(
          !is.na(total_patents_euro) & total_patents_euro > 0 & total_patents > 0 ~ "USPTO + Europe",
          !is.na(total_patents_euro) & total_patents_euro > 0 ~ "Europe Only",
          TRUE ~ patent_source
        )
      ) %>%
      select(-ends_with("_euro"))
    cat(sprintf("   ✅ Merged European patent data for %d companies\n", nrow(european_patents)))
  }
} else {
  cat("   No European patent data to merge\n")
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.7 Load Bloomberg cached data
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n📈 Loading Bloomberg monthly prices from cache...\n")
bloomberg_prices <- tryCatch({
  load_bloomberg_cache()
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("   ⚠️ Bloomberg cache not available:", e$message, "\n")
  cat("   Continuing without Bloomberg data...\n")
  return(NULL)
})
if (!is.null(bloomberg_prices)) {
  cat(sprintf("   ✅ Loaded Bloomberg data for %d companies\n", length(unique(bloomberg_prices$ticker))))
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.7.1 Pull MSCI data (optional)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
msci_data <- NULL
cat("\n📊 MSCI data not available (optional, skipping)\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1.8 Create master dataset (one row per company)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🔄 Creating master company snapshot...\n")
master_snapshot <- create_company_snapshot(
  company_map = company_map,
  rd_history = rd_history,
  current_holdings = current_holdings,
  daily_ratios = daily_ratios,
  patent_data = patent_data
)
master_snapshot <- calculate_company_features(master_snapshot)
master_snapshot <- handle_missing_values(master_snapshot)

if (!is.null(bloomberg_prices)) {
  bloomberg_summary <- bloomberg_prices %>%
    group_by(ticker) %>%
    summarise(
      bloomberg_volatility = first(volatility_12m),
      total_return_5yr = last(price) / first(price) - 1,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    rename(Ticker = ticker)
  master_snapshot <- master_snapshot %>%
    left_join(bloomberg_summary, by = "Ticker")
}

master_snapshot <- master_snapshot %>%
  group_by(Ticker) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Master snapshot created: %d companies\n", nrow(master_snapshot)))
cat(sprintf("   📊 In portfolio: %d\n", sum(master_snapshot$in_portfolio)))
cat(sprintf("   📊 Discovery universe: %d\n", sum(!master_snapshot$in_portfolio)))
snapshot_file <- here("data", "02_processed", "live_company_snapshot.rds")
saveRDS(master_snapshot, snapshot_file)
cat("\n✅ Live snapshot saved to:", snapshot_file, "\n")

daii_raw_data <- master_snapshot

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 2: LOAD HOLDINGS DATA (DUMAC PORTFOLIO)
# =============================================================================
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🏦 SECTION 2: LOADING DUMAC HOLDINGS DATA\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.1 Process holdings (ISIN + name matching)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
process_holdings_data_fixed <- function(holdings_path = "data/01_raw/current_holdings.rds",
                                        company_map_input = NULL) {
  message("   Processing DUMAC holdings data...")
  holdings_data <- readRDS(holdings_path)
  holdings_aggregated <- holdings_data$firm_aggregated
  message(sprintf("   Raw holdings: %d securities", nrow(holdings_aggregated)))
  holdings_all_isins <- holdings_aggregated %>%
    filter(!is.na(ISIN)) %>%
    mutate(
      clean_name = gsub(" - CLASS.*$| INC\\.?$| LTD\\.?$| CORP\\.?$| CO\\.?$", "", security_name),
      clean_name = trimws(clean_name),
      clean_name = toupper(clean_name)
    )
  message(sprintf("   Securities with ISINs: %d (%.1f%%)", nrow(holdings_all_isins),
                  nrow(holdings_all_isins) / nrow(holdings_aggregated) * 100))
  company_lookup <- company_map_input %>%
    mutate(
      lookup_name = toupper(gsub(" Inc$| Corp$| Ltd$| LLC$| PLC$| Co$| Company$", "", CompanyName)),
      lookup_name = trimws(lookup_name)
    )
  message(sprintf("   Company universe: %d companies", nrow(company_lookup)))
  matches_isin <- company_lookup %>%
    left_join(holdings_all_isins, by = "ISIN", relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
    group_by(Ticker) %>%
    summarise(
      total_net_exposure_usd = sum(total_net_exposure_usd, na.rm = TRUE),
      total_net_pct_ltp = sum(total_net_pct_ltp, na.rm = TRUE),
      n_funds = sum(n_funds, na.rm = TRUE),
      matched_by = "ISIN",
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  isin_match_count <- sum(matches_isin$total_net_exposure_usd > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  message(sprintf("   ISIN matches: %d companies", isin_match_count))
  if(nrow(company_lookup) > 0 && nrow(holdings_all_isins) > 0) {
    companies_without_isin_match <- matches_isin %>%
      filter(total_net_exposure_usd == 0 | is.na(total_net_exposure_usd)) %>%
      pull(Ticker)
    unmatched_companies <- company_lookup %>%
      filter(Ticker %in% companies_without_isin_match)
    matched_isin_tickers <- company_lookup %>%
      filter(Ticker %in% matches_isin$Ticker[matches_isin$total_net_exposure_usd > 0]) %>%
      pull(ISIN)
    holdings_for_name_match <- holdings_all_isins %>%
      filter(!ISIN %in% matched_isin_tickers)
    matches_name <- holdings_for_name_match %>%
      inner_join(unmatched_companies, by = c("clean_name" = "lookup_name"), relationship = "many-to-many") %>%
      group_by(Ticker) %>%
      summarise(
        total_net_exposure_usd = sum(total_net_exposure_usd, na.rm = TRUE),
        total_net_pct_ltp = sum(total_net_pct_ltp, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_funds = sum(n_funds, na.rm = TRUE),
        matched_by = "NAME",
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    message(sprintf("   Name matches: %d companies", nrow(matches_name)))
    matches_isin <- matches_isin %>%
      left_join(matches_name, by = "Ticker", suffix = c("", "_name")) %>%
      mutate(
        total_net_exposure_usd = ifelse(is.na(total_net_exposure_usd_name), total_net_exposure_usd, total_net_exposure_usd_name),
        total_net_pct_ltp = ifelse(is.na(total_net_pct_ltp_name), total_net_pct_ltp, total_net_pct_ltp_name),
        n_funds = ifelse(is.na(n_funds_name), n_funds, n_funds_name),
        matched_by = ifelse(is.na(matched_by_name), matched_by, "NAME")
      ) %>%
      select(-ends_with("_name"))
  } else {
    message("   Name matches: 0 companies")
  }
  total_portfolio <- sum(matches_isin$total_net_exposure_usd, na.rm = TRUE)
  holdings_lookup <- matches_isin %>%
    mutate(
      total_net_exposure_usd = ifelse(is.na(total_net_exposure_usd), 0, total_net_exposure_usd),
      total_net_pct_ltp = ifelse(is.na(total_net_pct_ltp), 0, total_net_pct_ltp),
      n_funds = ifelse(is.na(n_funds), 0, n_funds),
      matched_by = ifelse(is.na(matched_by), "NONE", matched_by),
      in_portfolio = total_net_exposure_usd != 0,
      fund_weight = ifelse(in_portfolio, total_net_exposure_usd / total_portfolio, 0)
    )
  message(sprintf("\n   ✅ HOLDINGS SUMMARY:"))
  message(sprintf("      Total companies in universe: %d", nrow(holdings_lookup)))
  message(sprintf("      Companies WITH holdings: %d", sum(holdings_lookup$in_portfolio)))
  message(sprintf("      Companies WITHOUT holdings: %d", sum(!holdings_lookup$in_portfolio)))
  message(sprintf("      Total portfolio value: $%s", format(total_portfolio, big.mark = ",")))
  message(sprintf("\n   Matching methods:"))
  message(sprintf("      ISIN matches: %d", sum(holdings_lookup$matched_by == "ISIN")))
  message(sprintf("      Name matches: %d", sum(holdings_lookup$matched_by == "NAME")))
  message(sprintf("      Unmatched: %d", sum(holdings_lookup$matched_by == "NONE")))
  return(holdings_lookup)
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2.2 Create holdings lookup
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
holdings_lookup <- process_holdings_data_fixed(
  holdings_path = "data/01_raw/current_holdings.rds",
  company_map_input = company_map
)
cat(sprintf("\n   ✅ Holdings processed: %d companies total, %d with positions\n", 
            nrow(holdings_lookup), sum(holdings_lookup$in_portfolio)))

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 3: CREATE MASTER TICKER LIST
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📋 SECTION 3: CREATING MASTER TICKER LIST\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
master_tickers <- unique(c(rd_history$ticker, holdings_lookup$Ticker))
cat(sprintf("   Master list: %d unique companies\n", length(master_tickers)))

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 4: LOAD PRE‑FETCHED PANEL DATA
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📈 SECTION 4: LOADING PANEL DATA\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
company_data <- master_snapshot
cat(sprintf("   Using master snapshot with %d companies\n", nrow(company_data)))

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 5: CREATE COMPANY SNAPSHOT (ONE ROW PER TICKER)
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📊 SECTION 5: CREATING COMPANY SNAPSHOT\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5.1 Deduplication (already deduplicated upstream)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
snapshot <- company_data
cat("   Snapshot loaded with", nrow(snapshot), "rows (already deduplicated)\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5.2 Add fund_weight (fallback for non‑portfolio companies)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
snapshot <- snapshot %>%
  mutate(
    fund_weight = ifelse(in_portfolio & !is.na(total_net_pct_ltp) & total_net_pct_ltp > 0,
                         total_net_pct_ltp / 100,
                         1 / n())
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5.3 Calculate revenue growth from historical rd_history data
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n📈 Calculating revenue growth from historical data...\n")
snapshot$revenue_growth <- NA_real_
if(exists("rd_history") && nrow(rd_history) > 0) {
  revenue_growth_calc <- rd_history %>%
    filter(!is.na(revenue) & revenue > 0) %>%
    filter(revenue > 100) %>%
    arrange(Ticker, fiscal_year_end) %>%
    group_by(Ticker) %>%
    mutate(
      revenue_previous = lag(revenue),
      revenue_growth_calc = case_when(
        !is.na(revenue) & !is.na(revenue_previous) & revenue_previous > 0 ~ 
          (revenue - revenue_previous) / revenue_previous,
        TRUE ~ NA_real_
      )
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(revenue_growth_calc)) %>%
    filter(revenue_growth_calc < 10) %>%
    slice(n()) %>%
    select(Ticker, revenue_growth_calc)
  if(nrow(revenue_growth_calc) > 0) {
    for(i in 1:nrow(revenue_growth_calc)) {
      ticker_val <- revenue_growth_calc$Ticker[i]
      growth_val <- revenue_growth_calc$revenue_growth_calc[i]
      snapshot$revenue_growth[snapshot$Ticker == ticker_val] <- growth_val
    }
    snapshot$revenue_growth <- pmax(-1, pmin(1, snapshot$revenue_growth))
    growth_count <- sum(!is.na(snapshot$revenue_growth))
    cat(sprintf("   ✅ Revenue growth calculated for %d companies using rd_history\n", growth_count))
  } else {
    cat("   ⚠️ No revenue growth calculations available\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("   ⚠️ rd_history not found. Revenue growth will be NA.\n")
}
cat(sprintf("   📊 revenue_growth column has %d non-NA values\n", sum(!is.na(snapshot$revenue_growth))))
cat(sprintf("   ⚠️  Missing for %d companies\n", sum(is.na(snapshot$revenue_growth))))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5.4 Ensure revenue growth column is saved to CSV
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(!"revenue_growth" %in% names(snapshot)) {
  cat("   ⚠️ revenue_growth column missing! Adding placeholder.\n")
  snapshot$revenue_growth <- NA_real_
}
cat(sprintf("   ✅ revenue_growth column ready for save (%d non-NA)\n", sum(!is.na(snapshot$revenue_growth))))
raw_dir <- here("data", "raw")
snapshot_file <- file.path(raw_dir, "N245_company_snapshot_FIXED.csv")
write.csv(snapshot, snapshot_file, row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✅ Snapshot saved to:", snapshot_file, "\n")
cat("   Columns saved:", ncol(snapshot), "\n")
cat("   revenue_growth included:", "revenue_growth" %in% names(snapshot), "\n")
cat("\n📊 FINAL COMPOSITION:\n")
cat(sprintf("   Total companies: %d\n", nrow(snapshot)))
cat(sprintf("   In portfolio: %d\n", sum(snapshot$in_portfolio)))
cat(sprintf("   Discovery universe: %d\n", sum(!snapshot$in_portfolio)))
cat(sprintf("   With R&D data: %d\n", sum(!is.na(snapshot$rd_expense))))
cat(sprintf("   With patent data: %d\n", sum(snapshot$patent_activity > 0, na.rm = TRUE)))
cat(sprintf("   With revenue growth: %d\n", sum(!is.na(snapshot$revenue_growth))))

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 6: PROCEED TO MAIN PIPELINE (LOAD SNAPSHOT, ENSURE COLUMNS)
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🚀 SECTION 6: READY FOR MAIN PIPELINE\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
cat("✅ DATA PREPARATION COMPLETE\n")
cat("   Loading snapshot for main pipeline...\n")
daii_raw_data <- read.csv(snapshot_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
cat(sprintf("   ✅ Snapshot loaded: %d rows × %d columns\n", nrow(daii_raw_data), ncol(daii_raw_data)))
if(!"revenue_growth" %in% names(daii_raw_data)) {
  cat("   ⚠️ revenue_growth column missing from loaded CSV!\n")
  if(exists("snapshot") && "revenue_growth" %in% names(snapshot)) {
    if("Ticker" %in% names(snapshot)) {
      snapshot_subset <- snapshot %>% select(Ticker, revenue_growth)
      daii_raw_data <- daii_raw_data %>%
        left_join(snapshot_subset, by = "Ticker")
      cat("   ✅ revenue_growth column added via left_join (using Ticker)\n")
    }
  }
}
growth_available <- sum(!is.na(daii_raw_data$revenue_growth))
cat(sprintf("   📊 revenue_growth available for %d companies\n", growth_available))
if("Ticker" %in% names(daii_raw_data) && !"ticker" %in% names(daii_raw_data)) {
  daii_raw_data <- daii_raw_data %>% rename(ticker = Ticker)
  cat("\n   Renamed Ticker to ticker for innovation scoring\n")
}
cat("\n✅ DATA READY FOR INNOVATION SCORING\n")

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 7: INNOVATION SCORING
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📈 SECTION 7: INNOVATION SCORING\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7.1 Ensure industry column
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(!"industry" %in% names(daii_raw_data)) {
  if("TRBC_Industry" %in% names(daii_raw_data)) {
    daii_raw_data$industry <- daii_raw_data$TRBC_Industry
  } else {
    daii_raw_data$industry <- "Unknown"
  }
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7.2 Calculate innovation scores
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
source(here("R", "04_modules", "01_innovation_scoring.r"))
daii_scored <- calculate_innovation_scores(daii_raw_data)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7.3 Deduplicate
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🔄 CRITICAL: Deduplicating to one row per company...\n")
cat("   Before dedupe:", nrow(daii_scored), "rows\n")
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  group_by(ticker) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()
cat("   After dedupe:", nrow(daii_scored), "unique companies\n")

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 8: MERGE HOLDINGS DATA (ADD PORTFOLIO FLAGS)
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🏦 SECTION 8: MERGING DUMAC HOLDINGS DATA\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# Ensure daii_scored has a 'ticker' column (use 'Ticker' if present)
if(!"ticker" %in% names(daii_scored) && "Ticker" %in% names(daii_scored)) {
  daii_scored <- daii_scored %>% rename(ticker = Ticker)
}

if(exists("holdings_lookup") && nrow(holdings_lookup) > 0) {
  holdings_lookup_clean <- holdings_lookup %>% rename(ticker = Ticker)
  daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
    left_join(holdings_lookup_clean, by = "ticker") %>%
    mutate(
      in_portfolio = ifelse(is.na(in_portfolio.y), FALSE, in_portfolio.y),
      n_funds = ifelse(is.na(n_funds.y), 0, n_funds.y),
      fund_weight = ifelse(is.na(fund_weight.y), 0, fund_weight.y),
      total_net_exposure_usd = ifelse(is.na(total_net_exposure_usd.y), 0, total_net_exposure_usd.y),
      total_net_pct_ltp = ifelse(is.na(total_net_pct_ltp.y), 0, total_net_pct_ltp.y)
    ) %>%
    select(-ends_with(".x"), -ends_with(".y"))
}
if(!"industry" %in% names(daii_scored)) daii_scored$industry <- "Unknown"

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 9: AI INTENSITY SCORING & PORTFOLIO CONSTRUCTION
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🤖 SECTION 9: AI INTENSITY SCORING & PORTFOLIO CONSTRUCTION\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9.1 Industry multipliers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
industry_multipliers <- data.frame(
  industry = c("Semiconductors & Semiconductor","Software & Services","Media & Entertainment",
               "Pharmaceuticals, Biotechnology","Technology Hardware & Equipmen","Automobiles & Components",
               "Financial Services","Capital Goods","Consumer Discretionary Distrib","Energy",
               "Materials","Utilities","Unknown"),
  multiplier = c(1.5,1.4,1.3,1.2,1.2,1.1,0.8,0.9,1.0,0.7,0.8,0.5,1.0)
)

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9.2 AI score and label
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  left_join(industry_multipliers, by = "industry") %>%
  mutate(
    multiplier = ifelse(is.na(multiplier), 1.0, multiplier),
    ai_score = innovation_score * multiplier,
    ai_quartile = ntile(ai_score, 4),
    ai_label = case_when(
      ai_quartile == 4 ~ "AI Leader",
      ai_quartile == 3 ~ "AI Adopter",
      ai_quartile == 2 ~ "AI Follower",
      TRUE ~ "AI Laggard"
    )
  )
cat("\n🤖 AI Score Distribution:\n")
print(table(daii_scored$ai_label))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9.3 DUMAC portfolios
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n💼 Building DUMAC Portfolios...\n")
portfolio_vol_median <- median(daii_scored$volatility[daii_scored$in_portfolio], na.rm = TRUE)
total_portfolio_weight <- sum(daii_scored$fund_weight[daii_scored$in_portfolio], na.rm = TRUE)
dumac_portfolios <- daii_scored %>%
  filter(in_portfolio == TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    quality_innovators_weight = ifelse(innovation_quartile == 4 & volatility < portfolio_vol_median,
                                       fund_weight / total_portfolio_weight, 0),
    ai_concentrated_weight = ifelse(ai_quartile == 4, fund_weight / total_portfolio_weight, 0),
    balanced_growth_weight = ifelse(innovation_quartile >= 3 & ai_quartile >= 3,
                                    fund_weight / total_portfolio_weight, 0)
  )
cat(sprintf("   DUMAC Portfolio Companies: %d\n", nrow(dumac_portfolios)))
cat(sprintf("   Quality Innovators: %d\n", sum(dumac_portfolios$quality_innovators_weight > 0)))
cat(sprintf("   AI Concentrated: %d\n", sum(dumac_portfolios$ai_concentrated_weight > 0)))
cat(sprintf("   Balanced Growth: %d\n", sum(dumac_portfolios$balanced_growth_weight > 0)))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 9.4 Discovery portfolios
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🔍 Building Discovery Portfolios...\n")
discovery_portfolios <- daii_scored %>%
  filter(in_portfolio == FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    innovation_rank = rank(-innovation_score, ties.method = "first"),
    ai_rank = rank(-ai_score, ties.method = "first"),
    combined_rank = rank(-(innovation_score + ai_score), ties.method = "first"),
    discovery_quality_weight = ifelse(
      innovation_quartile == 4 & volatility < median(volatility, na.rm = TRUE),
      innovation_score / sum(innovation_score[innovation_quartile == 4 & volatility < median(volatility, na.rm = TRUE)], na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    discovery_ai_weight = ifelse(ai_quartile == 4,
                                 ai_score / sum(ai_score[ai_quartile == 4], na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    discovery_tier = case_when(
      combined_rank <= 10 ~ "Tier 1: Top 10 Opportunities",
      combined_rank <= 25 ~ "Tier 2: Strong Candidates",
      combined_rank <= 50 ~ "Tier 3: Watch List",
      TRUE ~ "Tier 4: Monitor"
    )
  )
cat(sprintf("   Discovery Universe Companies: %d\n", nrow(discovery_portfolios)))
cat("\n📊 Discovery Tiers:\n")
print(table(discovery_portfolios$discovery_tier))

daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  left_join(dumac_portfolios[, c("ticker", "quality_innovators_weight", "ai_concentrated_weight", 
                                 "balanced_growth_weight")], by = "ticker") %>%
  left_join(discovery_portfolios[, c("ticker", "discovery_quality_weight", "discovery_ai_weight", 
                                     "discovery_tier", "innovation_rank", "ai_rank", "combined_rank")], 
            by = "ticker") %>%
  mutate(across(ends_with("_weight"), ~ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .)))

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 10: AI CUBE & ANOMALY DETECTION
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🧠 SECTION 10: AI CUBE & ANOMALY DETECTION\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10.1 Strategic profiles
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
ai_cube <- daii_scored %>%
  mutate(
    strategic_profile = case_when(
      ai_quartile == 4 & innovation_quartile == 4 ~ "AI Pioneer",
      ai_quartile == 4 & innovation_quartile <= 2 ~ "AI Focused",
      ai_quartile <= 2 & innovation_quartile == 4 ~ "Innovation Leader",
      ai_quartile >= 3 & innovation_quartile >= 3 ~ "Balanced Performer",
      TRUE ~ "Underperformer"
    ),
    ai_exposure = case_when(ai_quartile == 4 ~ "High", ai_quartile == 3 ~ "Medium", TRUE ~ "Low"),
    innovation_exposure = case_when(innovation_quartile == 4 ~ "High", innovation_quartile == 3 ~ "Medium", TRUE ~ "Low")
  )
cat("📊 AI Exposure Cube Summary:\n")
print(table(ai_cube$strategic_profile))

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 10.2 Isolation Forest anomaly detection
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n🔍 Running anomaly detection...\n")
anomaly_features <- ai_cube %>%
  select(rd_intensity, patent_activity, revenue_growth, volatility, 
         market_cap, innovation_score, ai_score) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.infinite(.) | is.na(.), 0, .)))
anomaly_features_scaled <- scale(anomaly_features) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ifelse(is.na(.), 0, .)))
if (require(isotree, quietly = TRUE) && nrow(anomaly_features_scaled) > 10) {
  set.seed(42)
  iso_model <- isolation.forest(
    data = anomaly_features_scaled,
    ntrees = 100,
    sample_size = min(256, nrow(anomaly_features_scaled)),
    ndim = ncol(anomaly_features_scaled),
    seed = 42
  )
  ai_cube$anomaly_score <- predict(iso_model, anomaly_features_scaled)
} else {
  anomaly_scores <- sapply(anomaly_features_scaled, function(x) abs(x - mean(x)) / sd(x))
  ai_cube$anomaly_score <- rowMeans(anomaly_scores, na.rm = TRUE)
}
ai_cube$anomaly_score <- (ai_cube$anomaly_score - min(ai_cube$anomaly_score)) / 
  (max(ai_cube$anomaly_score) - min(ai_cube$anomaly_score))
anomaly_threshold <- quantile(ai_cube$anomaly_score, 0.9, na.rm = TRUE)
ai_cube$is_anomaly <- ai_cube$anomaly_score >= anomaly_threshold
cat(sprintf("\n   Anomalies detected: %d companies\n", sum(ai_cube$is_anomaly, na.rm = TRUE)))
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  left_join(ai_cube[, c("ticker", "strategic_profile", "ai_exposure", "innovation_exposure", 
                        "anomaly_score", "is_anomaly")], by = "ticker")
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  group_by(ticker) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup()

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 11: AGGRESSIVE AI ANALYSIS (UNICORN HUNTING)
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🦄 SECTION 11: AGGRESSIVE AI ANALYSIS – UNICORN HUNTING\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 11.1 Aggressive weights
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
weights_aggressive <- list(rd_weight = 0.25, patent_weight = 0.20, growth_weight = 0.40, vol_weight = 0.15)
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  mutate(
    rd_score_norm = rd_quartile * 25,
    patent_score_norm = patent_quartile * 25,
    growth_score_norm = growth_quartile * 25,
    stability_score_norm = (5 - volatility_quartile) * 25,
    ai_score_aggressive = rd_score_norm * weights_aggressive$rd_weight +
      patent_score_norm * weights_aggressive$patent_weight +
      growth_score_norm * weights_aggressive$growth_weight +
      stability_score_norm * weights_aggressive$vol_weight
  )
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  mutate(
    high_growth = revenue_growth >= quantile(revenue_growth, 0.7, na.rm = TRUE),
    high_volatility = volatility >= quantile(volatility, 0.7, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_rd = rd_intensity > 0 & !is.na(rd_intensity),
    not_ai_leader = ai_label != "AI Leader",
    high_potential_flag = high_growth & high_volatility & has_rd & not_ai_leader,
    unicorn_tier = case_when(
      high_potential_flag & ai_score_aggressive >= quantile(ai_score_aggressive, 0.8, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "Top Unicorn Candidate",
      high_potential_flag ~ "Emerging Unicorn Candidate",
      high_growth & has_rd & not_ai_leader ~ "Growth Candidate",
      TRUE ~ "Monitor"
    )
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 11.2 Unicorn tier assignment
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
aggressive_threshold <- quantile(daii_scored$ai_score_aggressive, 0.65, na.rm = TRUE)
daii_scored <- daii_scored %>%
  mutate(
    ai_label_aggressive = case_when(
      ai_score_aggressive >= aggressive_threshold ~ "AI Leader (Aggressive)",
      ai_score_aggressive >= quantile(ai_score_aggressive, 0.5, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "AI Adopter",
      ai_score_aggressive >= quantile(ai_score_aggressive, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE) ~ "AI Follower",
      TRUE ~ "AI Laggard"
    )
  )

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 11.3 Create unicorn watchlist
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
unicorn_watchlist <- daii_scored %>%
  filter(unicorn_tier %in% c("Top Unicorn Candidate", "Emerging Unicorn Candidate")) %>%
  arrange(desc(ai_score_aggressive)) %>%
  select(ticker, company_name, ai_score_aggressive, revenue_growth, volatility, 
         unicorn_tier, rd_intensity, patent_activity)
cat("\n🦄 UNICORN HUNTING SUMMARY:\n")
cat("   Standard AI Leaders:", sum(daii_scored$ai_label == "AI Leader"), "\n")
cat("   Aggressive AI Leaders:", sum(daii_scored$ai_label_aggressive == "AI Leader (Aggressive)"), "\n")
cat("   Top Unicorn Candidates:", sum(unicorn_watchlist$unicorn_tier == "Top Unicorn Candidate"), "\n")
cat("   Emerging Unicorn Candidates:", sum(unicorn_watchlist$unicorn_tier == "Emerging Unicorn Candidate"), "\n")
cat("   Total High-Potential Watchlist:", nrow(unicorn_watchlist), "\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Create portfolio_holdings for benchmark analysis (after unicorn_tier exists)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
if(exists("daii_scored") && "ai_score" %in% names(daii_scored)) {
  portfolio_holdings <- daii_scored %>%
    filter(in_portfolio == TRUE) %>%
    select(ticker, company_name, fund_weight, ai_score, ai_label, 
           innovation_score, revenue_growth, volatility, unicorn_tier)
  cat("   ✅ Created portfolio_holdings for benchmark analysis\n")
} else {
  cat("   ⚠️ Could not create portfolio_holdings – required columns missing\n")
}

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 12: BENCHMARK CONSTITUENTS & COMPARISON
# =============================================================================
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📊 SECTION 12: BENCHMARK CONSTITUENTS & COMPARISON\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 12.1 Fetch benchmark constituents with error handling
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
conn <- tryCatch({
  connect_research()
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("❌ Failed to connect to RESEARCH database. Benchmark constituents will be missing.\n")
  return(NULL)
})

get_index_constituents <- function(index_name, index_ric, conn) {
  if(is.null(conn)) return(character(0))
  sql <- sprintf("
    SELECT DISTINCT i.Xref_Ticker AS Ticker
    FROM refinitiv.class20_geninfo_comp_idx_membr im
    JOIN refinitiv.class20_refinfo_issue i ON im.RepNo = i.RepNo
    WHERE im.[Index] = '%s'
      AND im.IndexRIC = '%s'
      AND i.Xref_Ticker IS NOT NULL
  ", index_name, index_ric)
  result <- dbGetQuery(conn, sql)
  return(result$Ticker)
}

if (!is.null(conn)) {
  benchmarks_list <- list(
    "S&P 500" = list(name = "S&P 500", ric = ".INX", var_name = "sp500_tickers"),
    "NASDAQ 100" = list(name = "NASDAQ 100 Index", ric = ".NDX", var_name = "nasdaq100_tickers"),
    "S&P 400 Mid Cap" = list(name = "S&P 400 Mid Cap", ric = ".IDX", var_name = "sp400_mid_cap_tickers"),
    "S&P 600 Small Cap" = list(name = "S&P 600 Small Cap", ric = ".SPCY", var_name = "sp600_small_cap_tickers")
  )
  benchmark_constituents_list <- list()
  for(b in names(benchmarks_list)) {
    cat("Fetching", b, "...\n")
    tickers <- get_index_constituents(benchmarks_list[[b]]$name, benchmarks_list[[b]]$ric, conn)
    benchmark_constituents_list[[b]] <- tickers
    var_name <- benchmarks_list[[b]]$var_name
    assign(var_name, tickers, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    cat("   Found", length(tickers), "constituents\n")
  }
  dbDisconnect(conn)
} else {
  # Placeholders to avoid downstream errors
  sp500_tickers <- character(0)
  nasdaq100_tickers <- character(0)
  sp400_mid_cap_tickers <- character(0)
  sp600_small_cap_tickers <- character(0)
  benchmark_constituents_list <- list()
  cat("⚠️ Skipping benchmark constituent fetch due to connection error.\n")
}

cat("\n📊 BENCHMARK CONSTITUENTS SUMMARY:\n")
for(b in names(benchmark_constituents_list)) {
  cat("   ", b, ":", length(benchmark_constituents_list[[b]]), "companies\n")
}

benchmark_results <- data.frame()
for(benchmark in names(benchmark_constituents_list)) {
  tickers <- benchmark_constituents_list[[benchmark]]
  available <- tickers[tickers %in% daii_scored$ticker]
  if(length(available) > 0) {
    benchmark_data <- daii_scored %>%
      filter(ticker %in% available) %>%
      summarise(
        benchmark = benchmark,
        n_constituents = length(tickers),
        n_covered = length(available),
        coverage_pct = round(length(available) / length(tickers) * 100, 1),
        avg_ai_score = mean(ai_score, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_ai_score = median(ai_score, na.rm = TRUE),
        pct_ai_leaders = mean(ai_label == "AI Leader", na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    benchmark_results <- bind_rows(benchmark_results, benchmark_data)
  }
}
portfolio_data <- daii_scored %>%
  summarise(
    benchmark = "DUMAC Portfolio",
    n_constituents = NA,
    n_covered = n(),
    coverage_pct = NA,
    avg_ai_score = mean(ai_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_ai_score = median(ai_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_ai_leaders = mean(ai_label == "AI Leader", na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
benchmark_comparison <- bind_rows(portfolio_data, benchmark_results)
cat("\n📊 BENCHMARK COMPARISON:\n")
print(benchmark_comparison)
assign("benchmark_comparison", benchmark_comparison, envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("benchmark_constituents_list", benchmark_constituents_list, envir = .GlobalEnv)

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 13: BENCHMARK AI EXPOSURE ANALYSIS
# =============================================================================
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📊 SECTION 13: BENCHMARK AI EXPOSURE ANALYSIS\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
source("R/04_modules/06_benchmark_ai_exposure.r")
benchmark_constituents_for_ai <- list(
  "S&P 500" = sp500_tickers,
  "NASDAQ 100" = nasdaq100_tickers,
  "S&P 400 Mid Cap" = sp400_mid_cap_tickers,
  "S&P 600 Small Cap" = sp600_small_cap_tickers
)
benchmark_ai_results <- list()
for(bench_name in names(benchmark_constituents_for_ai)) {
  cat(sprintf("   Calculating AI exposure for %s...\n", bench_name))
  benchmark_ai_results[[bench_name]] <- calculate_benchmark_ai_exposure(
    benchmark_constituents_for_ai[[bench_name]],
    daii_scored,
    weights = "market_cap"
  )
}
ai_exposure_comparison <- compare_ai_exposure(
  portfolio_holdings,
  benchmark_constituents_for_ai,
  daii_scored
)
cat("\n   Calculating sector AI exposure for S&P 500...\n")
sp500_sector_ai <- calculate_sector_ai_exposure(
  benchmark_constituents_for_ai[["S&P 500"]],
  daii_scored
)
radar_data <- create_ai_radar_data(
  ai_exposure_comparison[ai_exposure_comparison$name == "DUMAC Portfolio", ],
  benchmark_ai_results
)
cat("\n📊 AI EXPOSURE COMPARISON:\n")
print(ai_exposure_comparison)
cat("\n📊 S&P 500 AI EXPOSURE BY SECTOR (Top 10):\n")
print(head(sp500_sector_ai, 10))
assign("ai_exposure_comparison", ai_exposure_comparison, envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("sp500_sector_ai", sp500_sector_ai, envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("radar_data", radar_data, envir = .GlobalEnv)
assign("benchmark_ai_results", benchmark_ai_results, envir = .GlobalEnv)
cat("\n   ✅ Benchmark AI exposure analysis complete\n")

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 14: CONTROL BENCHMARKS (AI‑LOW, MATCHED‑PAIR)
# =============================================================================
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📊 SECTION 14: CONTROL BENCHMARKS\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")

module_file <- "R/04_modules/08_control_benchmarks.r"
if(file.exists(module_file)) {
  source(module_file)
  if(exists("daii_scored") && exists("portfolio_holdings")) {
    portfolio_holdings_enhanced <- portfolio_holdings %>%
      left_join(daii_scored %>% select(ticker, market_cap), by = "ticker")
    cat("\n🤖 Creating AI-Low benchmark...\n")
    ai_low_benchmark <- construct_ai_low_benchmark(
      daii_scored = daii_scored,
      portfolio_weights = portfolio_holdings,
      n_components = 100
    )
    cat(sprintf("   AI-Low benchmark: %d companies\n", nrow(ai_low_benchmark)))
    cat("\n🔗 Creating matched-pair controls...\n")
    matched_controls <- construct_matched_controls(
      portfolio_companies = portfolio_holdings_enhanced,
      universe = daii_scored,
      match_vars = c("market_cap", "revenue_growth"),
      k = 1,
      method = "nearest"
    )
    if(nrow(matched_controls$pairs) > 0) {
      cat(sprintf("\n   ✅ Matched pairs created: %d\n", nrow(matched_controls$pairs)))
      cat(sprintf("   Average AI gap: %.3f\n", matched_controls$quality$avg_ai_gap))
    }
    cat("\n🧠 Preparing donor pool for synthetic control...\n")
    donor_pool <- prepare_donor_pool(
      daii_scored = daii_scored,
      exclude_tickers = portfolio_holdings$ticker,
      ai_threshold = 0.3
    )
    cat(sprintf("   Donor pool: %d companies\n", length(donor_pool)))
    assign("ai_low_benchmark", ai_low_benchmark, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("matched_controls", matched_controls, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign("donor_pool", donor_pool, envir = .GlobalEnv)
  }
} else {
  cat(sprintf("   ❌ Module not found: %s\n", module_file))
}
cat("\n   ✅ Benchmark analysis complete\n")

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 15: PERFORMANCE ATTRIBUTION
# =============================================================================
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("📈 SECTION 15: PERFORMANCE ATTRIBUTION\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
source("R/04_modules/05_performance_attribution.r")
attribution_results <- NULL
if("as_of_date" %in% names(daily_ratios) && "price_1d_pct_change" %in% names(daily_ratios)) {
  benchmarks_present <- c(".SPX", ".IXIC") %in% unique(daily_ratios$Ticker)
  if(all(benchmarks_present)) {
    benchmark_returns <- daily_ratios %>%
      filter(Ticker %in% c(".SPX", ".IXIC")) %>%
      select(date = as_of_date, ticker = Ticker, daily_return = price_1d_pct_change) %>%
      arrange(ticker, date)
    holdings_weights <- snapshot %>%
      left_join(holdings_lookup, by = c("ticker" = "Ticker")) %>%
      filter(in_portfolio == TRUE) %>%
      select(Ticker = ticker, fund_weight)
    returns_data <- daily_ratios %>%
      select(date = as_of_date, ticker = Ticker, daily_return = price_1d_pct_change) %>%
      filter(!is.na(daily_return))
    attribution_results <- calculate_performance_attribution(
      holdings_data = holdings_weights,
      benchmark_data = benchmark_returns %>% filter(ticker == ".SPX") %>% select(date, benchmark_return = daily_return),
      return_data = returns_data,
      start_date = "2020-01-01",
      end_date = Sys.Date()
    )
    if(!is.null(attribution_results)) {
      benchmark_comparison <- create_benchmark_comparison(snapshot, attribution_results)
      cat("\n📊 BENCHMARK COMPARISON:\n")
      print(benchmark_comparison)
    }
  }
}
assign("attribution_results", attribution_results, envir = .GlobalEnv)

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 16: UNIVERSAL CONTROL PANEL (OPTIONAL)
# =============================================================================
cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("🎯 SECTION 16: UNIVERSAL CONTROL PANEL (OPTIONAL)\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
cat("   ⚠️ Universal control panel is disabled (optional feature).\n")
cat("   ✅ Core control benchmarks (AI-Low, Matched-Pair, Power Analysis) available in Section 14.\n")

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 17: OUTPUT GENERATION
# =============================================================================
output_dir <- "outputs"
if(!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
cat("📁 Output directory:", output_dir, "\n")
run_timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
run_dir <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("run_", run_timestamp))
dir.create(run_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
cat("📁 Run directory:", run_dir, "\n")

write.csv(daii_scored, file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_01_company_features_full.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(daii_scored[, c("ticker", "ai_score", "innovation_score", "ai_quartile", 
                          "innovation_quartile", "ai_label", "innovation_label", "in_portfolio")], 
          file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_02_ai_scores.csv")), row.names = FALSE)

dumac_only <- daii_scored %>% filter(in_portfolio == TRUE)
if(nrow(dumac_only) > 0) {
  write.csv(dumac_only[dumac_only$quality_innovators_weight > 0, 
                       c("ticker", "quality_innovators_weight", "innovation_score", "volatility")],
            file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_03_dumac_quality_innovators.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(dumac_only[dumac_only$ai_concentrated_weight > 0,
                       c("ticker", "ai_concentrated_weight", "ai_score")],
            file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_04_dumac_ai_concentrated.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(dumac_only[dumac_only$balanced_growth_weight > 0,
                       c("ticker", "balanced_growth_weight", "innovation_score", "ai_score")],
            file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_05_dumac_balanced_growth.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}

discovery_only <- daii_scored %>% filter(in_portfolio == FALSE)
if(nrow(discovery_only) > 0) {
  write.csv(discovery_only[discovery_only$discovery_quality_weight > 0,
                           c("ticker", "discovery_quality_weight", "innovation_score", "volatility", "discovery_tier")],
            file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_07_discovery_quality_innovators.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(discovery_only[discovery_only$discovery_ai_weight > 0,
                           c("ticker", "discovery_ai_weight", "ai_score", "discovery_tier")],
            file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_08_discovery_ai_concentrated.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(discovery_only %>% arrange(combined_rank) %>%
              select(ticker, innovation_score, ai_score, discovery_tier,
                     innovation_rank, ai_rank, combined_rank,
                     discovery_quality_weight, discovery_ai_weight),
            file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_10_discovery_full_universe.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}

write.csv(daii_scored[, c("ticker", "anomaly_score", "is_anomaly", "ai_score", 
                          "innovation_score", "in_portfolio")],
          file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_11_anomaly_scores_full.csv")), row.names = FALSE)

unicorn_watchlist <- daii_scored %>%
  filter(unicorn_tier %in% c("Top Unicorn Candidate", "Emerging Unicorn Candidate")) %>%
  arrange(desc(ai_score_aggressive)) %>%
  select(ticker, company_name, ai_score_aggressive, revenue_growth, volatility, 
         unicorn_tier, rd_intensity, patent_activity, market_cap)
write.csv(unicorn_watchlist, file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_18_unicorn_watchlist.csv")), row.names = FALSE)

if(exists("ai_exposure_comparison")) {
  write.csv(ai_exposure_comparison, file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_23_benchmark_ai_exposure.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}
if(exists("sp500_sector_ai")) {
  write.csv(sp500_sector_ai, file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_24_sp500_sector_ai_exposure.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}
if(exists("radar_data")) {
  write.csv(radar_data, file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_25_ai_radar_data.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}

if(exists("portfolio_holdings") && nrow(portfolio_holdings) > 0) {
  concentration_data <- portfolio_holdings %>%
    arrange(desc(fund_weight)) %>%
    mutate(rank = row_number(), cumulative_weight = cumsum(fund_weight), weight_pct = round(fund_weight * 100, 2))
  write.csv(concentration_data, file.path(run_dir, paste0(run_timestamp, "_20_portfolio_concentration.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
}

cat("\n✅✅✅ ALL OUTPUTS GENERATED SUCCESSFULLY\n")
cat("   Run directory:", run_dir, "\n")
cat("   Output files:", length(list.files(run_dir)), "\n")
cat("🏁 PIPELINE EXECUTION COMPLETE\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
```

## Copilot

Yes — this newer version is **much better** for the plan.

## Direct answer

**Yes, the reviewer-support plan is feasible against this updated script.**  
More importantly, it is now **clean enough to implement sequentially**.

### Why this version is better
Compared with the older version:
- Sections 1–3 are now in the right order
- the duplicate early holdings block is gone
- `company_map` is built before Section 2 uses it
- Section boundaries are clearer
- the checkpoint targets are straightforward:
  - Section 1 → `company_map`, `rd_history`, `master_snapshot`
  - Section 2 → `holdings_lookup`
  - Section 3 → `master_tickers`

So now we can proceed with **complete code integrations**, one item at a time.

# Recommended implementation strategy

We should still keep the changes minimal:

## Additions we need
1. **review-mode helper block** near the top of the script
2. **N=25 filtering support** in Section 1
3. **Section 1 checkpoint saves**
4. **Section 2 checkpoint saves**
5. **Section 3 checkpoint saves**
6. **stop-after-section logic**
7. optional wrapper script after the main file works

## Important design choice
For speed, I now recommend:

- **put the helper block directly into the pipeline script first**
- do **not** split into a helper file yet

That is the fastest way to get to a working test.

---

# Code Item 1 — Complete review-support block

## Where to put it
Insert this **right after the library loads** and **before Section 1 starts**.

So it goes after:

```r
library(dplyr)
library(magrittr)
library(DBI)
library(odbc)
library(here)
```

and before:

```r
# =============================================================================
# SECTION 1: DATA INTEGRATION (LIVE DATA SOURCES)
# =============================================================================
```

## Insert this full code block

```r
# =============================================================================
# REVIEW SUPPORT MODE
# =============================================================================
# Lightweight reviewer-support features for Sprint 1 Sections 1-3.
# These additions are intended to improve observability and validation only.
# They should not change core business logic when review mode is off.

RUN_REVIEW_MODE <- identical(tolower(Sys.getenv("RUN_REVIEW_MODE", "false")), "true")
STOP_AFTER_SECTION <- Sys.getenv("STOP_AFTER_SECTION", "")

review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if (RUN_REVIEW_MODE && exists("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- get("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

review_log <- function(...) {
  cat("[REVIEW MODE]", ..., "\n")
}

setup_review_output_dir <- function() {
  run_timestamp_review <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
  base_dir <- here::here("outputs", "section_checkpoints", paste0("run_", run_timestamp_review))
  dir.create(base_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  return(base_dir)
}

save_review_checkpoint <- function(obj, file_path, format = c("csv", "rds")) {
  format <- match.arg(format)
  dir.create(dirname(file_path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  if (format == "csv") {
    write.csv(obj, file_path, row.names = FALSE)
  } else if (format == "rds") {
    saveRDS(obj, file_path)
  }
}

write_validation_summary <- function(obj, file_path, obj_name,
                                     critical_cols = NULL,
                                     unique_key = NULL) {
  lines <- c(
    paste("Validation summary for:", obj_name),
    paste("Timestamp:", Sys.time()),
    paste("Rows:", if (!is.null(nrow(obj))) nrow(obj) else NA),
    paste("Columns:", paste(colnames(obj), collapse = ", "))
  )

  if (!is.null(critical_cols)) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("Missing critical columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    } else {
      lines <- c(lines, "Missing critical columns: none")
    }

    present_cols <- intersect(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    for (col in present_cols) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("NA count for", col, ":", sum(is.na(obj[[col]]))))
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(unique_key) && unique_key %in% colnames(obj)) {
    dup_count <- sum(duplicated(obj[[unique_key]]))
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Duplicate", unique_key, "count:", dup_count))
  }

  writeLines(lines, con = file_path)
}

stop_if_review_section_complete <- function(section_num) {
  if (RUN_REVIEW_MODE &&
      nzchar(STOP_AFTER_SECTION) &&
      identical(as.character(section_num), STOP_AFTER_SECTION)) {
    review_log(paste("Stopping after section", section_num))
    stop(paste("REVIEW_STOP_SECTION", section_num))
  }
}

review_output_dir <- NULL
if (RUN_REVIEW_MODE) {
  review_output_dir <- setup_review_output_dir()
  review_log(paste("Reviewer output directory:", review_output_dir))
}
```

---

After this insertion, the pipeline can support:
- review mode on/off
- section stop flags
- reviewer output folder creation
- checkpoint file writing
- validation summaries

This is your foundation.
