# Actual code skeletons

Below are the starter versions I would use.

---

## 1) `R/01_utils/review_support.R`

```r name=R/01_utils/review_support.R
is_review_mode <- function() {
  identical(tolower(Sys.getenv("RUN_REVIEW_MODE", "false")), "true")
}

get_stop_after_section <- function() {
  Sys.getenv("STOP_AFTER_SECTION", "")
}

setup_review_output_dir <- function() {
  run_timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
  base_dir <- here::here("outputs", "section_checkpoints", paste0("run_", run_timestamp))
  dir.create(base_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  return(base_dir)
}

review_log <- function(...) {
  cat("[REVIEW MODE]", ..., "\n")
}

save_review_checkpoint <- function(obj, path, format = c("csv", "rds")) {
  format <- match.arg(format)
  dir.create(dirname(path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  if (format == "csv") {
    write.csv(obj, path, row.names = FALSE)
  } else if (format == "rds") {
    saveRDS(obj, path)
  }
}

write_validation_summary <- function(obj,
                                     file_path,
                                     obj_name,
                                     critical_cols = NULL,
                                     unique_key = NULL) {
  lines <- c(
    paste("Validation summary for:", obj_name),
    paste("Timestamp:", Sys.time())
  )

  if (!is.null(dim(obj))) {
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Rows:", nrow(obj)))
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Columns:", paste(colnames(obj), collapse = ", ")))
  } else {
    lines <- c(lines, "Object is not a data frame / tabular object")
  }

  if (!is.null(critical_cols) && !is.null(colnames(obj))) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    if (length(missing_cols) == 0) {
      lines <- c(lines, "Missing critical columns: none")
    } else {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("Missing critical columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    }

    for (col in intersect(critical_cols, colnames(obj))) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("NA count for", col, ":", sum(is.na(obj[[col]]))))
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(unique_key) && !is.null(colnames(obj)) && unique_key %in% colnames(obj)) {
    dupes <- sum(duplicated(obj[[unique_key]]))
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Duplicate", unique_key, "count:", dupes))
  }

  writeLines(lines, con = file_path)
}

stop_if_review_section_complete <- function(section_num) {
  stop_after <- get_stop_after_section()

  if (is_review_mode() &&
      nzchar(stop_after) &&
      identical(as.character(section_num), stop_after)) {
    review_log("Stopping after section", section_num)
    stop(
      structure(
        list(message = paste("REVIEW_STOP_SECTION", section_num)),
        class = c("review_stop", "condition")
      )
    )
  }
}
```

---

## 2) `run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

```r name=run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R
library(here)
library(utils)

Sys.setenv(RUN_REVIEW_MODE = "true")
Sys.setenv(STOP_AFTER_SECTION = "3")

n25_path <- here::here("data", "00_reference", "n25_representative_sample.csv")
n25_df <- read.csv(n25_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if ("ticker" %in% names(n25_df)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- unique(n25_df$ticker)
} else if ("Ticker" %in% names(n25_df)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- unique(n25_df$Ticker)
} else {
  stop("N=25 sample file must contain a 'ticker' or 'Ticker' column.")
}

assign("review_sample_tickers", review_sample_tickers, envir = .GlobalEnv)

tryCatch(
  {
    source(here::here("DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r"))
    cat("Review run completed.\n")
  },
  review_stop = function(e) {
    message("Review-mode stop reached as expected: ", e$message)
  }
)
```

---

# Minimal canonical-script changes

These are the **must-have** edits in the main script.

## Near the top of the canonical script
Add:

```r
library(here)

source(here("R", "01_utils", "review_support.R"))

review_mode <- is_review_mode()
review_output_dir <- NULL

if (review_mode) {
  review_output_dir <- setup_review_output_dir()
  review_log("Reviewer output directory:", review_output_dir)
}

review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if (review_mode && exists("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- get("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)
}
```

---

## After `company_map` is built
Add something like:

```r
if (review_mode && !is.null(review_sample_tickers)) {
  ticker_col <- if ("Ticker" %in% colnames(company_map)) "Ticker" else if ("ticker" %in% colnames(company_map)) "ticker" else NULL

  if (!is.null(ticker_col)) {
    company_map <- company_map[company_map[[ticker_col]] %in% review_sample_tickers, ]
    review_log("Filtered company_map to review sample. Rows:", nrow(company_map))
  }
}

if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  save_review_checkpoint(company_map, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_company_map.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    company_map,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_company_map_validation.txt"),
    "company_map",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker", "ISIN"),
    unique_key = "Ticker"
  )
}
```

You may need to adjust `critical_cols` if the actual case/column names differ.

---

## After `rd_history` is built
Add:

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  rd_sample <- utils::head(rd_history, 100)
  save_review_checkpoint(rd_sample, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_rd_history_sample.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    rd_history,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_rd_history_validation.txt"),
    "rd_history",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker", "revenue"),
    unique_key = NULL
  )
}
```

---

## After `master_snapshot` is ready
Add:

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  save_review_checkpoint(master_snapshot, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot.rds"), "rds")
  save_review_checkpoint(master_snapshot, file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    master_snapshot,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot_validation.txt"),
    "master_snapshot",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker"),
    unique_key = "Ticker"
  )
}

stop_if_review_section_complete(1)
```

---

## After `holdings_lookup` is ready in Section 2
Add:

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section2_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section2")
  save_review_checkpoint(holdings_lookup, file.path(section2_dir, "section2_holdings_lookup.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    holdings_lookup,
    file.path(section2_dir, "section2_holdings_lookup_validation.txt"),
    "holdings_lookup",
    critical_cols = c("Ticker"),
    unique_key = "Ticker"
  )
}

stop_if_review_section_complete(2)
```

---

## After `master_tickers` is ready in Section 3
Add:

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section3_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section3")
  master_tickers_df <- data.frame(ticker = master_tickers, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  save_review_checkpoint(master_tickers_df, file.path(section3_dir, "section3_master_ticker_list.csv"), "csv")
  write_validation_summary(
    master_tickers_df,
    file.path(section3_dir, "section3_master_ticker_list_validation.txt"),
    "master_ticker_list",
    critical_cols = c("ticker"),
    unique_key = "ticker"
  )
}

stop_if_review_section_complete(3)
```

# Important warning before coding

## The duplicate / premature Section 2 block
Based on the earlier inspection, the canonical script appears to have:
- holdings-related execution near the top
- before `company_map` is properly built
- and then a later Section 2 block again

That means before relying on the wrapper, you should either:

### Safer quick fix
Guard the early duplicate block in review mode:

```r
if (!review_mode) {
  # old early holdings block
}
```

### Better long-term fix
Remove/refactor the duplicate block entirely after confirming the later Section 2 block is the canonical one.

For tomorrow morning, I would do the **guard**, not a full deletion.

# Developer issue / task ticket

Here is the cleaned internal ticket text.

````markdown
# Add reviewer-support execution mode for Sprint 1 Sections 1–3

## Summary
Implement lightweight reviewer-support changes so Sprint 1 Sections 1–3 can be executed on the N=25 sample with checkpoint outputs and validation summaries, without changing underlying business logic.

## Background
Current Sprint 1 review is organized into section-level PRs, but the canonical script is a monolithic integrated pipeline. Reviewers need a reliable way to:
- run the Sprint 1 scope on the N=25 sample
- inspect section-level outputs
- validate key intermediate objects
- document decomposition findings in PRs

Current issues:
- key outputs are mostly in-memory objects
- plain `source()` runs the full file unless review-mode stopping logic is added
- script ordering appears inconsistent in early holdings logic
- reviewers currently lack stable section-level output artifacts

## Goal
Add a review execution mode for Sprint 1 Sections 1–3 that:
- runs on the N=25 sample
- saves reviewer-friendly checkpoint outputs
- writes validation summaries
- stops cleanly after Section 1, 2, or 3 as configured
- does not alter underlying section business logic

## Deliverables

### 1. Review-support helper file
Create:
- `R/01_utils/review_support.R`

Include helper functions for:
- review-mode detection
- reviewer output directory setup
- checkpoint saving
- validation summary writing
- section-stop handling

### 2. Canonical script review-mode support
Update:
- `DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r`

Add:
- helper sourcing
- review-mode initialization
- N=25 sample filtering support
- checkpoint saves after Sections 1–3
- clean stop-after-section logic

Also guard or remove the premature top-of-file holdings execution block so review-mode execution is consistent.

### 3. Reviewer wrapper script
Create:
- `run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

This wrapper should:
- enable review mode
- load the N=25 sample
- expose sample tickers to the main script
- source the canonical script
- stop after Section 3
- print success/output-location messaging

### 4. Reviewer checkpoint outputs
Implement checkpoint outputs for:
- Section 1:
  - company mapping object
  - historical R&D sample/object summary
  - integrated snapshot object
- Section 2:
  - holdings lookup output
- Section 3:
  - master ticker list

### 5. Validation summaries
Add reviewer-readable validation summaries including:
- row counts
- schema/column presence
- missingness for critical fields
- unique-key / duplicate-key checks
- file existence checks
- warnings/errors where relevant

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Review mode can be enabled with environment variables or equivalent configuration
- [ ] Wrapper runs the canonical script through Section 3 on the N=25 sample
- [ ] Section 1 checkpoint files are written successfully
- [ ] Section 2 checkpoint files are written successfully
- [ ] Section 3 checkpoint files are written successfully
- [ ] Validation summary files are created for each section
- [ ] Review-mode execution does not continue into downstream sections when stop-after-section is set
- [ ] Changes do not alter underlying business logic or downstream production behavior
- [ ] Pilot run is completed and outputs are reviewed before reviewer release

## Notes
This work is intended to improve reviewer observability and validation only. It should not expand PR approval scope or change governance boundaries for Sprint 1 section reviews.
````

More concretely, from the snippets you pasted earlier, we saw:

2. the output of `git branch --show-current`.

## @sganesan_dumac

I've uploaded it to "dev" for the review. At some point, I will need to remove "DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026" in DAII-Framework-2026 repo under "main," which has old code. 

## Copilot

Yes — that makes sense.

## Direct recommendation
**Do not remove the old file from `main` yet.**

Since you’ve now uploaded the true review file to `dev`, the safest sequence is:

1. **use the new true-review file for current review work**
2. **update PRs/reviewer guidance to point to the new file**
3. **confirm the old 5.3 file is no longer needed**
4. **remove the old file later in a separate cleanup PR**

That separation is important because:
- you are in active review setup now
- deleting the old file too early could break references or confuse reviewers
- cleanup/removal should be a distinct, auditable change

---

# Recommended plan

## Phase 1 — right now
Treat the new file on `dev` as the active review artifact.

### Do now
- update the PR bodies/comments to reference:
  - `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026`
  - or `...r` if that is the real filename
- stop telling reviewers to use the old 5.3 file
- verify the new file is the one you want reviewed

---

## Phase 2 — after review setup is stable
Open a dedicated cleanup PR to remove the old file from `main`.

That PR should be explicitly framed as:
- removing outdated canonical candidate
- reducing reviewer confusion
- preserving the newer true-review file as the active artifact

---

# Important caution
If the old file is referenced anywhere in:
- PR descriptions
- review manifests
- wrapper scripts
- docs
- comments
- project board notes

then update those references **before** deleting it.

Otherwise reviewers will click dead references or run the wrong file.

---

# Best practice for removing it later

## Safer approach
Do the removal in a branch and PR, not directly on `main`.

### Example flow
When the time comes:

```bash
git checkout main
git pull
git checkout -b cleanup/remove-old-5-3-review-script
git rm "DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026"
git commit -m "Remove outdated 5.3 review pipeline file"
git push -u origin cleanup/remove-old-5-3-review-script
```

If the file actually has `.r`, then use:

```bash
git rm "DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r"
```

Then open a PR into `main`.

---

# My recommendation on timing

## Do not delete it yet if:
- reviewers have already seen that filename
- PRs still reference it
- docs still reference it
- you have not yet validated the new true-review file

## Delete it when:
- all Sprint 1 PRs point to the new file
- your decomposition addenda point to the new file
- any wrapper/test scripts point to the new file
- the team is aligned that the old file is superseded

---

# What I would do next
1. confirm the exact filename of the new true-review file on `dev`
2. update all reviewer-facing materials to reference it
3. then plan a later cleanup PR to remove the old one from `main`

---

# One more important nuance
If you want historical traceability, instead of simply deleting the old file immediately, you could:
- move it to `legacy/`
- or `R/99_archive/`
- or another archive location

That can be better than outright deletion if:
- you want an audit trail
- you may need to compare old vs true-review versions
- reviewers might ask what changed

So your two options later are:

## Option A — remove it entirely
Cleaner repo, less confusion.

## Option B — archive it
Better traceability, slightly more clutter.

For your situation, I’d lean toward **archive first, remove later if still unnecessary**.

If you want, I can next help you with:
1. the exact text to update in your PRs to point to the new true-review file, and  
2. a cleanup plan for eventually removing or archiving the old 5.3 file.

## Copilot

Yes — here is the **full practical work plan** to get the Sprint 1 review live.

I’m assuming:

- the first reviewer-facing scope is still **Sprint 1 Sections 1–3**,
- and your goal is to get reviewers into GitHub PRs with enough structure to review code, run N=25 where possible, and support decomposition.

# Master checklist to get the review up and running

## Phase 0 — Lock the correct review artifact
Before anything else, make sure everyone is reviewing the right file.

### Must complete
- [ ] confirm the **exact filename** of the new true-review pipeline file, including extension
- [ ] confirm it is committed/pushed to `dev`
- [ ] stop using the old 5.3 filename in reviewer-facing materials
- [ ] identify whether the old file will be:
  - [ ] temporarily retained
  - [ ] archived later
  - [ ] removed later in a separate cleanup PR

### Deliverable
One agreed canonical review file for Sprint 1.

---

## Phase 1 — Update all reviewer-facing references
Everything reviewers see should point to the new true-review file.

### Must update
- [ ] Section 1 PR body
- [ ] Section 2 PR body
- [ ] Section 3 PR body
- [ ] decomposition addendum comments
- [ ] N=25 run comments
- [ ] reviewer instructions if they mention the old file
- [ ] any review manifests in `review/sprint-1/`
- [ ] any session summary or runtime-entrypoint notes that are still being used operationally
- [ ] any wrapper/test scripts that reference the old file

### Deliverable
No active review artifact points to the wrong pipeline file.

---

## Phase 2 — Finalize the 3 Sprint 1 PRs
The PRs need to be short, scoped, and reviewer-friendly.

### For each of the 3 PRs
- [ ] confirm title is correct
- [ ] replace long/convoluted body with compact version
- [ ] confirm in-scope region is correct
- [ ] confirm out-of-scope list is correct
- [ ] confirm Jira key is correct
- [ ] confirm the canonical file reference uses the new true-review file

### Target PRs
- [ ] `Sprint 1 / Section 1 / Data Integration`
- [ ] `Sprint 1 / Section 2 / Load Holdings Data`
- [ ] `Sprint 1 / Section 3 / Create Master Ticker List`

### Deliverable
Three clean, readable, section-scoped draft PRs.

---

## Phase 3 — Add PR conversation comments
The PR body should stay short. The rest goes in comments.

### Add to each PR
- [ ] reviewer instructions comment
- [ ] section-specific decomposition addendum comment
- [ ] N=25 reviewer run and validation comment

### Deliverable
Each PR has the 3 supporting comment blocks reviewers need.

---

## Phase 4 — Build section-to-files review inventories
You need to make it easy for reviewers to know what files matter.

### For Section 1
- [ ] identify canonical script region
- [ ] identify supporting ingest/transform files
- [ ] identify key functions/logic areas
- [ ] identify input data files
- [ ] identify expected outputs/intermediate objects
- [ ] identify critical fields/columns

### For Section 2
- [ ] identify holdings-related supporting files
- [ ] identify holdings input data and mapping objects
- [ ] identify critical functions
- [ ] identify output objects/files
- [ ] identify critical match/merge keys

### For Section 3
- [ ] identify master ticker list logic and supporting files
- [ ] identify upstream inputs from Sections 1–2
- [ ] identify key dedup/matching fields
- [ ] identify output object/file
- [ ] identify downstream dependency expectations

### Deliverable
A usable section-to-files map for Sections 1–3.

---

## Phase 5 — Decide and implement reviewer-support code changes
This is the most important technical enablement step.

## Goal
Make reviewers able to inspect section-level outputs without manually probing in-memory objects.

### Must decide
- [ ] are Sections 1–3 still cumulative-only in the new true-review file?
- [ ] what checkpoint outputs do we want after Section 1?
- [ ] what checkpoint outputs do we want after Section 2?
- [ ] what checkpoint outputs do we want after Section 3?
- [ ] do we want one combined wrapper or separate wrappers?

### My recommendation
- [ ] implement **one reviewer wrapper first**
- [ ] add checkpoint outputs after Sections 1–3
- [ ] add validation summaries
- [ ] do not attempt full modular refactor yet

### Deliverable
A minimum viable reviewer-execution layer.

---

## Phase 6 — Create helper and wrapper files
These are the actual implementation artifacts.

### Create / update
- [ ] `R/01_utils/review_support.R`
- [ ] `run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

### Helper file should support
- [ ] review-mode detection
- [ ] output directory creation
- [ ] checkpoint saving
- [ ] validation summary writing
- [ ] clean stop-after-section behavior

### Wrapper should support
- [ ] enabling review mode
- [ ] loading N=25 sample
- [ ] exposing sample tickers to the main script
- [ ] sourcing the true-review pipeline file
- [ ] stopping after Section 3
- [ ] printing output locations

### Deliverable
A runnable review-support harness.

---

## Phase 7 — Modify the true-review pipeline file minimally
The wrapper alone won’t work unless the pipeline script cooperates.

### Must add
- [ ] source the helper file early
- [ ] initialize review mode
- [ ] initialize reviewer output directory
- [ ] load/use `review_sample_tickers`
- [ ] save Section 1 checkpoints
- [ ] save Section 2 checkpoints
- [ ] save Section 3 checkpoints
- [ ] stop cleanly after Section 1/2/3 as configured

### Must inspect
- [ ] whether there is still an ordering issue at the top of the corrected file
- [ ] whether an early duplicate holdings/Section 2 block still exists
- [ ] whether the corrected file still mixes `source(here(...))` and `source("R/...")`
- [ ] whether the new file uses the same data paths as before

### Deliverable
The true-review pipeline can run in reviewer mode.

---

## Phase 8 — Create section-level checkpoint outputs
Reviewers need inspectable files.

### Section 1 checkpoint targets
- [ ] company mapping checkpoint
- [ ] R&D history sample/summary
- [ ] integrated snapshot checkpoint
- [ ] Section 1 validation summary

### Section 2 checkpoint targets
- [ ] holdings lookup checkpoint
- [ ] holdings validation summary

### Section 3 checkpoint targets
- [ ] master ticker list checkpoint
- [ ] ticker validation summary

### Deliverable
Concrete files reviewers can inspect.

---

## Phase 9 — Define validation checks
Reviewer output needs lightweight quality checks.

### Add checks for each checkpoint
- [ ] row count
- [ ] schema/column presence
- [ ] missingness on critical fields
- [ ] unique key / duplicate key detection
- [ ] join completeness where applicable
- [ ] file existence check
- [ ] warning/error capture

### Deliverable
Validation summaries that are readable and useful.

---

## Phase 10 — Pilot test Section 1 before broad release
Do not send reviewers into all 3 PRs before you test the experience.

### Pilot steps
- [ ] use the wrapper locally
- [ ] run through at least Section 1
- [ ] confirm checkpoint files are created
- [ ] confirm validation summaries are readable
- [ ] confirm the PR instructions match reality
- [ ] confirm a reviewer could locate the correct outputs
- [ ] confirm the new true-review file is the one actually being run

### Must answer
- [ ] can a reviewer identify the in-scope code region?
- [ ] can a reviewer identify the supporting files?
- [ ] can a reviewer run the code with available prerequisites?
- [ ] can a reviewer find the output?
- [ ] can a reviewer tell what to validate?

### Deliverable
A tested reviewer flow for Section 1.

---

## Phase 11 — Update PRs with real execution details
Once the pilot works, replace placeholders with real instructions.

### Update in PR comments
- [ ] exact command/script to run
- [ ] exact required data file paths
- [ ] exact expected output file names
- [ ] known limitations
- [ ] whether execution is cumulative-only or partially section-scoped

### Deliverable
PR comments that match actual tested behavior.

---

## Phase 12 — Confirm reviewer access readiness
Being assigned in GitHub is not enough by itself.

### Confirm reviewers have
- [ ] repo access
- [ ] local/runtime environment access
- [ ] N=25 sample access
- [ ] supporting file access
- [ ] any needed credentials/secrets
- [ ] awareness of where to leave comments

### Deliverable
No reviewer is blocked by missing access surprises.

---

## Phase 13 — Send reviewer communication
Once the PRs and pilot are ready, send the team message.

### Must send
- [ ] Teams message/email
- [ ] explain scope is section-limited
- [ ] tell them where to comment
- [ ] tell them how to run N=25 validation
- [ ] tell them what to document in decomposition comments
- [ ] explain that GitHub PRs are the technical evidence record

### Deliverable
Reviewers know what to do.

---

## Phase 14 — Move PRs from setup to active review
Once tested and communicated:

### Do
- [ ] confirm PRs are still draft or move them to ready as appropriate
- [ ] re-request reviews if needed
- [ ] update project board status
- [ ] monitor first reviewer feedback
- [ ] refine instructions if first reviewers are confused

### Deliverable
Active review begins.

---

If you want the shortest “must-have” list, it is this:

- [ ] correct true-review file identified and referenced everywhere
- [ ] 3 PRs cleaned up
- [ ] reviewer instructions added
- [ ] decomposition addenda added
- [ ] N=25 comments added
- [ ] file/function/supporting-file inventory done for Sections 1–3
- [ ] minimal reviewer-support code changes implemented
- [ ] wrapper created
- [ ] Section 1 pilot tested
- [ ] actual run instructions updated in PR
- [ ] reviewer access confirmed
- [ ] Teams message sent

---

# What can wait until later

These are important, but not blockers for Sprint 1 launch:

- [ ] removing the old 5.3 file from `main`
- [ ] full runtime path normalization
- [ ] archiving all legacy root scripts
- [ ] deeper `src/` consolidation
- [ ] full repo restructuring
- [ ] perfect modularization of the monolithic pipeline

```
# Section 1 Review Guide for DAII 3.5 Pipeline

Below is a structured analysis of **Section 1 (Data Integration)** of the canonical script. It includes trace paths, decomposition tasks, and line‑item requirements. All content is ready to copy into your review documentation.

---

## Section 1: Data Integration (Live Data Sources)

### Reviewer Should Trace

| Trace Path | What to Follow | Why |
|------------|----------------|-----|
| **1. Data lineage of `company_map`** | From `build_company_resolution()` → filtering (if review mode) → checkpoint save. | Confirm that the company universe matches the expected 217 (or 25 in review mode) and that critical fields (Ticker, ISIN, Country, TRBC_Industry) are populated. |
| **2. R&D history pull** | `pull_rd_history(company_map)` → `rd_history` → sample checkpoint. | Verify that 40 years of revenue and R&D expense are retrieved and that no large gaps exist. |
| **3. Patent data flow** | USPTO bulk check → `pull_patent_data()` → European merge → final `patent_data`. | Ensure AI patents are correctly identified (CPC codes) and that the merge with European patents does not introduce duplicates. |
| **4. Master snapshot construction** | `create_company_snapshot()` → `calculate_company_features()` → `handle_missing_values()` → `master_snapshot`. | Validate that all data sources are joined correctly, that deduplication works, and that imputation flags are set. |

### Reviewer Decomposition Tasks

- **Task 1 – Verify company resolution**  
  Run the script up to `company_map` creation. Check that `company_map` contains the expected tickers (e.g., NVDA, MSFT, AAPL) and that `ISIN` is not missing for DUMAC holdings.

- **Task 2 – Inspect R&D history sample**  
  After `rd_history` is created, examine the first and last few years for a subset of companies. Confirm that `revenue` and `rd_expense` are non‑negative and that no obvious outliers (e.g., zero revenue with positive R&D) exist without imputation.

- **Task 3 – Validate patent classification**  
  Run the patent block and examine `patent_data`. Check that `ai_patents` > 0 for known AI leaders (NVIDIA, Microsoft) and that the `patent_source` column correctly reflects “USPTO Only” or “USPTO + Europe”.

- **Task 4 – Test imputation logic**  
  After `handle_missing_values()`, review the imputation flags (`rd_imputed`, `growth_imputed`, `volatility_imputed`, `innovation_imputed`). For at least one company that had imputed R&D, confirm that the imputed value is reasonable (e.g., not negative).

---

## Line‑Item Requirements for Section 1

The following table lists each logical block within Section 1, its requirements, and what the reviewer must inspect.

| Block | Requirements | Reviewer Inspection |
|-------|--------------|----------------------|
| **1.1 Source ingestion scripts** | All required scripts exist in `R/02_ingest/` and `R/03_transform/`. No errors when sourced. | Check that `source()` calls do not produce warnings (e.g., missing files). |
| **1.2 Build company resolution table** | Function `build_company_resolution()` must:  
- Connect to Refinitiv  
- Return a data frame with columns `Ticker`, `CompanyName`, `ISIN`, `Country`, `TRBC_Industry`  
- Have no duplicate `Ticker` rows  
- In review mode, filter to N=25 sample if `review_sample_tickers` is set | Run `head(company_map)`. Verify that `ISIN` is not NA for at least 90% of rows. Check that `TRBC_Industry` is populated. |
| **1.3 Pull 40‑year R&D history** | `pull_rd_history(company_map)` must:  
- Retrieve data for all companies in `company_map`  
- Include columns `Ticker`, `fiscal_year_end`, `rd_expense`, `revenue`  
- Return at least 20 years of history for most tickers | Inspect `rd_history` for a sample ticker (e.g., AAPL). Confirm that `fiscal_year_end` goes back to at least 2000. |
| **1.4 Pull current holdings** | `pull_current_holdings()` must return raw holdings data (from DUMAC warehouse). At minimum, columns `ISIN`, `security_name`, `total_net_exposure_usd`. | No specific review needed here – will be validated in Section 2. |
| **1.5 Pull daily ratios** | `pull_daily_ratios(company_map, start_date = "2024-01-01")` must return columns `Ticker`, `as_of_date`, `price_1d_pct_change`, `volatility_260d`. | Check that `volatility_260d` is between 0 and 1 for most companies. |
| **1.6 Pull patent data (USPTO + Europe)** | - USPTO bulk files are downloaded/updated if missing or older than 90 days.  
- `pull_patent_data()` returns columns `ticker`, `total_patents`, `ai_patents`, `patent_source`.  
- European patents are merged correctly. | Verify that `ai_patents` for NVDA > 0. Check that `patent_source` is not “USPTO Only” for companies with European patents. |
| **1.7 Load Bloomberg cache** | Optional – pipeline continues if cache missing. If present, `bloomberg_prices` should be a data frame with `ticker`, `volatility_12m`, `price`. | If Bloomberg data is used, ensure that `bloomberg_volatility` is added to `master_snapshot` without breaking existing columns. |
| **1.8 Create master snapshot** | `create_company_snapshot()` joins all ingested data into `master_snapshot`. Then:  
- `calculate_company_features()` adds derived columns (`rd_intensity`, `patent_activity`, `volatility`).  
- `handle_missing_values()` imputes missing values and sets imputation flags.  
- Final deduplication (`group_by(Ticker) %>% slice(1)`) guarantees one row per ticker. | Run `summary(master_snapshot)` and note any columns with >50% missingness. Check that `rd_imputed` is TRUE for companies that had missing R&D. |
| **Review checkpoints (review mode only)** | After each major object, the script saves CSV/RDS files and validation summaries in `outputs/section_checkpoints/run_<timestamp>/section1/`. | Confirm that all checkpoint files exist and that validation summaries contain row counts, column names, and missingness info. |
| **Stop after Section 1 (review mode only)** | If `STOP_AFTER_SECTION = "1"`, the script stops after saving checkpoints. | Run with that variable set; verify that execution halts and no later sections are executed. |

---

## How to Use This Guide

1. **Copy the entire block above** into your PR description or review issue.
2. **During the review meeting**, walk through each “Reviewer Inspection” column using the live script and N=25 sample.
3. **Log any deviations** as defects (see severity guidelines).

Once you have reviewed Section 1 and are satisfied, let me know and I will produce the **same detailed analysis for Section 2 (Load Holdings Data)** and **Section 3 (Create Master Ticker List)**.
```
1. DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r ("dev" in DAII-Framework-2026) -> old file ("DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r) true version in repo "DAII-3.5-Framework (main)" -> "DAII_3.5_TEST_232_COMPANIES_FINALFIXED_VERSION_5.3_WITH_DATA_INGESTION_INTEGRATION_3_18_2026.r" in DAII-Framework-2026 (main) is FALSE but currently is in protected branch so we will remove later.

2. Update all reviewer-facing references -> completed
3. Finalize the 3 Sprint 1 PRs -> completed
4. Add PR conversation comments - completed
5. For Section 1 decomposition addendum - Data Integration -> integrate the responses attached into the current addendum format so that reviewers can easily work through the addendum, suggestions, files etc...Provide your feedback where necessary to these responses and based on your examination of the DAII_3.5_TRUE_...script.




## Copilot

Yes — this is a **good substantive draft**, but I would **tighten it before putting it in front of reviewers**.

My overall assessment is:

- **Strong as working analysis**
- **Too definitive in a few places unless verified directly in `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r` and its sourced files**
- **Best used as a section-specific review guide / decomposition addendum**
- **Needs a few edits to avoid overclaiming and to make it easier for reviewers to work through**

Since you asked me to **integrate this into the current addendum format** and **provide feedback where necessary**, here’s my recommended version.

---

# Main feedback on your current Section 1 guide

## What is strong
- It is structured and reviewer-oriented.
- The “Reviewer Should Trace” table is very good.
- The “Reviewer Decomposition Tasks” are actionable.
- The line-item requirements are the right idea.
- It moves beyond generic process and into real review guidance.

## What I would change
### 1. Use the **new true review filename**
Everywhere this should now reference:

- `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`

not the old 5.3 file.

### 2. Soften anything that looks **unverified**
A few examples likely need verification against the new file or sourced modules before stating as facts:
- exact function names like `pull_current_holdings()`
- exact output expectations like “40 years”
- exact column requirements like “must return”
- “217 (or 25 in review mode)” unless confirmed in this specific script/review mode
- any assumptions about checkpoint files already existing, unless you’ve implemented them

### 3. Separate:
- **current script behavior**
from
- **reviewer-support behavior you intend to add**

Right now the guide slightly blends them.

Example:
- “Review checkpoints (review mode only)” is fine **only if that code already exists**
- if not, it should be labeled **planned reviewer-support enhancement**

### 4. Make reviewer steps slightly more GitHub-friendly
The addendum should explicitly tell them:
- what to inspect in code
- what to inspect in outputs
- what to leave in PR comments

### 5. Keep it compact enough for PR use
This is strong, but if pasted raw into a PR comment it may be a bit heavy.  
I recommend a **PR-ready condensed version** plus optional longer internal note if needed.

---

# Recommended integrated addendum version

This version keeps your substance but makes it safer, clearer, and more usable in the PR.

````markdown
## Section 1 decomposition addendum — Data Integration

### Section identity
- **Sprint:** 1
- **Section:** 1
- **Section Name:** Data Integration
- **Canonical Review File:** `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`

### Scope focus
This section covers the Section 1 data-integration portion of the canonical review script, including upstream live/source data ingestion and the creation of the initial integrated data objects handed forward to later Sprint 1 sections.

### Reviewer should trace

| Trace path | What to follow | Why |
|------------|----------------|-----|
| **1. Company resolution / company map lineage** | Company-resolution logic → any review-mode filtering → resulting company map object/checkpoint. | Confirm the company universe is correct for the active run mode and that critical identifiers such as ticker and ISIN are populated. |
| **2. R&D history pull** | Historical R&D / revenue pull logic → resulting history object/sample output. | Verify that historical financial inputs are populated, structurally sound, and usable for downstream feature creation. |
| **3. Patent data flow** | Patent-source ingestion logic → merge/aggregation steps → resulting patent data object. | Ensure patent-related inputs are correctly classified and merged without introducing obvious duplication or missingness issues. |
| **4. Master snapshot construction** | Snapshot creation logic → feature calculation → missing-value handling → final integrated snapshot object. | Validate that data sources are joined correctly, deduplication works, and imputation/normalization behavior is visible to reviewers. |

### Reviewer decomposition tasks
Please document in PR comments:

- what data enters Section 1
- what transformations occur before handoff to later sections
- what outputs leave Section 1
- what fields are materially transformed
- what dependencies or assumptions are hidden
- what should be validated before Section 2 review begins

Suggested reviewer tasks:
1. **Verify company resolution**
   - inspect the company mapping output
   - confirm key identifiers such as ticker and ISIN are populated at acceptable coverage
   - note obvious duplication or missing classification fields

2. **Inspect historical financial inputs**
   - inspect a small sample of historical R&D / revenue rows
   - confirm fields are non-negative where expected
   - note missingness, suspicious zeros, or obvious outliers

3. **Validate patent data flow**
   - inspect whether known AI-relevant firms appear to have nonzero AI-patent activity where expected
   - note whether patent-source labeling/merge behavior appears reasonable

4. **Review imputation / normalization behavior**
   - inspect imputation flags or equivalent missing-data handling outputs
   - confirm that imputed values appear plausible and non-pathological

### Key supporting files / modules to inspect
| File/module | Purpose | Why reviewer should look at it |
|-------------|---------|--------------------------------|
| `R/02_ingest/01_company_resolution.r` | Company/entity/ticker resolution logic. | Correct mapping of identifiers affects all downstream joins. |
| `R/02_ingest/02_pull_rd_history.r` | Historical R&D / revenue pull logic. | Provides financial inputs used later in feature construction. |
| `R/02_ingest/03_pull_holdings.r` | Holdings-related upstream ingestion logic. | Important for understanding handoff into Section 2, even if full validation is deferred there. |
| `R/02_ingest/04_pull_daily_ratios.r` | Daily ratio / volatility-related upstream inputs. | Supports downstream feature generation and later scoring logic. |
| `R/02_ingest/05_patentsview.r` and related patent-source scripts | Patent-related upstream inputs. | Patent activity is a material upstream source for later analysis. |
| `R/03_transform/01_create_snapshot.r` | Snapshot construction logic. | Main integration point where source alignment and duplication risk appear. |
| Related transform / missing-value handling scripts used by the review file | Derived-field and imputation logic. | Reviewers should confirm what transformations occur before Section 2 begins. |

### Important outputs to inspect
- company mapping object (for example, `company_map` or equivalent)
- historical R&D / revenue object (for example, `rd_history` or equivalent)
- integrated snapshot object (for example, `master_snapshot` or equivalent)

### Important fields / columns to trace
- `Ticker` / `ticker`
- `ISIN`
- country / industry classification fields
- `rd_expense`
- `revenue`
- patent-related counts/flags
- imputation flags or equivalent missing-value indicators

### Line-item review requirements
| Block | Requirements / questions | Reviewer inspection |
|-------|--------------------------|---------------------|
| **1. Source ingestion scripts** | Required scripts should exist and load successfully. | Note any missing file, path, or sourcing problems. |
| **2. Company resolution** | Mapping output should contain expected identifier fields and avoid obvious duplicate ticker rows. | Inspect head/sample, missingness, and uniqueness of identifiers. |
| **3. Historical R&D / revenue pull** | Historical inputs should be structurally usable and reasonably populated. | Inspect sample rows for time coverage, missingness, and obvious anomalies. |
| **4. Holdings-related upstream ingestion** | Upstream holdings ingestion should prepare the handoff to Section 2 correctly. | Full business validation can be deferred to Section 2, but note early inconsistencies here. |
| **5. Daily ratios / market inputs** | Ratio/volatility inputs should appear structurally plausible. | Inspect sample values and obvious range/pathology issues. |
| **6. Patent data ingestion / merge** | Patent-source inputs should merge without obvious duplication or labeling issues. | Inspect sample rows and note suspicious patent-source behavior. |
| **7. Snapshot construction** | Integrated snapshot should join source inputs coherently and support one expected row per entity/ticker at handoff. | Inspect row counts, duplicate behavior, and major missingness patterns. |
| **8. Missing-value handling / imputation** | Missing-data handling should be visible, interpretable, and non-pathological. | Inspect imputation flags or equivalent fields and note questionable replacements. |

### Reviewer validation support note
If reviewer-support code modifications are implemented, reviewers should also verify:
- checkpoint files for Section 1 are written successfully
- validation summary files exist
- row-count/schema summaries are readable
- review-mode execution can stop after Section 1 when configured

If these review-support features are **not yet implemented**, reviewers should instead document what intermediate objects/outputs are available only in memory and what additional support would improve validation.

### Reviewer output
Please leave:
- **line comments in Files changed** for code-specific issues
- **Conversation comments** for:
  - decomposition findings
  - validation findings
  - dependency/order concerns
  - hidden reliance on global state
  - suggested follow-up questions for Section 2
````

---

# Why this version is better

## 1. It matches your current process better
It fits the current PR-comment format:
- reviewer-friendly
- decomposition-focused
- not too generic
- not too implementation-heavy

## 2. It avoids overclaiming
I softened places where the current text sounded like hard assertions unless directly verified in the new true-review script.

## 3. It distinguishes current behavior vs planned reviewer-support changes
That’s important because some checkpoint/stop-after-section behavior may still be planned rather than already present.

---

# Specific feedback on your original attached responses

Here are the main line-item comments.

## “expected 217 (or 25 in review mode)”
**Feedback:** good intent, but I would avoid putting a hard number unless confirmed against the new script and current review-mode filtering.

### Better
- “matches the expected active universe for normal or review mode”

---

## “40 years of revenue and R&D expense”
**Feedback:** fine if directly verified, otherwise soften.

### Better
- “historical revenue and R&D coverage sufficient for downstream feature generation”

---

## “No specific review needed here – will be validated in Section 2”
**Feedback:** I would soften this.

### Better
- “detailed holdings-business validation is deferred to Section 2, but obvious ingestion issues should still be noted here”

---

## “Verify that ai_patents for NVDA > 0”
**Feedback:** useful as a spot-check idea, but it is better phrased as an example than a hard requirement.

### Better
- “spot-check whether known AI-relevant firms show plausible nonzero AI-patent counts where expected”

---

## “volatility_260d between 0 and 1 for most companies”
**Feedback:** useful but somewhat brittle depending on data representation.

### Better
- “volatility-related fields should be numerically plausible and free of obvious range/pathology problems”

---

## “Review checkpoints (review mode only)”
**Feedback:** only include as active expectation if you have actually implemented them in the true-review file.

Otherwise label as:
- **planned reviewer-support enhancement**

---

# Recommended reformat

## 1) Replace `### Reviewer should trace` table with this

````markdown
### Reviewer should trace

#### 1. Company resolution / company map lineage
- **Follow:** company-resolution logic → any review-mode filtering → resulting company map object/checkpoint
- **Why it matters:** confirms that the company universe is correct for the active run mode and that critical identifiers such as ticker and ISIN are populated

#### 2. Historical R&D / revenue pull
- **Follow:** historical R&D / revenue pull logic → resulting history object/sample output
- **Why it matters:** verifies that historical financial inputs are populated, structurally sound, and usable for downstream feature creation

#### 3. Patent data flow
- **Follow:** patent-source ingestion logic → merge/aggregation steps → resulting patent data object
- **Why it matters:** ensures patent-related inputs are classified and merged without obvious duplication or missingness problems

#### 4. Master snapshot construction
- **Follow:** snapshot creation logic → feature calculation → missing-value handling → final integrated snapshot object
- **Why it matters:** validates that data sources are joined correctly, deduplication works, and imputation/normalization behavior is visible before handoff to later sections
````

### Why this is better
- easier to read in GitHub
- each trace path gets its own visual block
- reviewers can comment against each numbered item

````markdown
### Key supporting files / modules to inspect

#### `R/02_ingest/01_company_resolution.r`
- **Purpose:** company/entity/ticker resolution logic
- **Why inspect it:** correct mapping of identifiers affects all downstream joins

#### `R/02_ingest/02_pull_rd_history.r`
- **Purpose:** historical R&D / revenue pull logic

- **Why inspect it:** helps reviewers understand the handoff into Section 2, even if full holdings validation is deferred there

- **Why inspect it:** patent activity is a material upstream source for later analysis

#### `R/03_transform/01_create_snapshot.r`

- **Why inspect it:** this is the main integration point where source alignment and duplication risk appear

#### Related transform / missing-value handling scripts used by the review file
- **Purpose:** derived-field and imputation logic
- **Why inspect them:** reviewers should confirm what transformations occur before Section 2 begins
````

- easier for novice reviewers to follow

# Recommended reformat for `### Line-item review requirements`

````markdown
### Line-item review requirements

#### 1. Source ingestion scripts
- **Requirement / question:** required scripts should exist and load successfully
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - whether all expected source files are present
  - whether `source()` calls succeed without obvious path or missing-file issues
  - whether any warnings/errors appear during script loading

#### 2. Company resolution
- **Requirement / question:** company-resolution output should contain expected identifier fields and avoid obvious duplicate ticker rows
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - sample rows from the company mapping output
  - missingness in key identifier fields such as ticker and ISIN
  - whether obvious duplicates exist for primary entity/ticker keys
  - whether classification fields such as country/industry appear populated

#### 3. Historical R&D / revenue pull
- **Requirement / question:** historical financial inputs should be structurally usable and reasonably populated
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - sample rows for a few representative tickers
  - time coverage and continuity where visible
  - obvious missingness, suspicious zeros, or implausible values
  - whether downstream feature construction appears feasible from the data returned

#### 4. Holdings-related upstream ingestion
- **Requirement / question:** upstream holdings ingestion should prepare the handoff to Section 2 correctly
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - whether the upstream holdings object is created without obvious structural issues
  - whether key identifier fields needed later appear present
  - any early mismatches or schema concerns that may affect Section 2
- **Note:** full business validation of holdings logic is deferred to Section 2

#### 5. Daily ratios / market inputs
- **Requirement / question:** ratio/volatility-related inputs should appear structurally plausible
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - whether expected fields are present
  - whether sample numeric values look plausible
  - whether any obvious range/pathology issues appear in volatility or return-related fields

#### 6. Patent data ingestion / merge
- **Requirement / question:** patent-source inputs should merge without obvious duplication or labeling issues
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - whether patent-related outputs are created successfully
  - whether sample rows show plausible AI-patent counts
  - whether merge/source labeling appears reasonable
  - whether obvious duplication or missingness issues appear after merge

#### 7. Snapshot construction
- **Requirement / question:** the integrated snapshot should join source inputs coherently and support one expected row per entity/ticker at handoff
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - row counts before and after deduplication if visible
  - whether duplicate entity/ticker rows persist
  - whether major expected source fields survive into the integrated snapshot
  - whether missingness patterns suggest broken joins or incomplete integration

#### 8. Missing-value handling / imputation
- **Requirement / question:** missing-data handling should be visible, interpretable, and non-pathological
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - imputation flags or equivalent missing-value indicators
  - whether imputed values appear reasonable
  - whether missingness is silently hidden instead of surfaced
  - whether imputation behavior could materially affect later Sprint 1 sections

#### 9. Reviewer-support checkpoints and stop behavior
- **Requirement / question:** if reviewer-support code has been implemented, checkpoint files and stop-after-section behavior should work as intended
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - whether checkpoint files are written successfully
  - whether validation summary files exist and are readable
  - whether row-count/schema summaries are useful
  - whether review-mode execution stops correctly after Section 1 when configured

- **If reviewer-support code is not yet implemented:**
  - document which intermediate objects are only available in memory
  - note what support changes would improve reviewer validation
````

---

# Why this is better
- much easier to read in GitHub comments
- every item becomes a mini checklist
- clearer distinction between:
  - requirement
  - inspection task
  - special note

---

# Optional shorter version

If you want a tighter PR-comment version, use this:

````markdown
### Line-item review requirements

1. **Source ingestion scripts**
   - Confirm required scripts exist and load successfully.
   - Note any missing-file, path, warning, or sourcing issues.

2. **Company resolution**
   - Confirm expected identifier fields are present and obvious duplicate ticker rows are absent.
   - Inspect sample rows, missingness, and classification coverage.

3. **Historical R&D / revenue pull**
   - Confirm historical financial inputs are structurally usable and reasonably populated.
   - Inspect sample rows for missingness, suspicious zeros, and obvious anomalies.

4. **Holdings-related upstream ingestion**
   - Confirm upstream holdings ingestion creates usable handoff inputs for Section 2.
   - Note any schema or identifier issues visible at this stage.

5. **Daily ratios / market inputs**
   - Confirm ratio/volatility fields are present and numerically plausible.
   - Note obvious range/pathology issues.

6. **Patent data ingestion / merge**
   - Confirm patent-related outputs are created and merged without obvious duplication or labeling problems.
   - Inspect sample rows for plausibility.

7. **Snapshot construction**
   - Confirm source inputs are joined coherently and the integrated snapshot is structurally ready for handoff.
   - Inspect row counts, duplicate behavior, and missingness patterns.

8. **Missing-value handling / imputation**
   - Confirm missing-data handling is visible and interpretable.
   - Inspect imputation flags or equivalent indicators for plausibility.

9. **Reviewer-support checkpoints / stop behavior**
   - If review-support code is implemented, confirm checkpoints and stop-after-section behavior work.
   - If not, document what remains in memory only and what support changes would help reviewers.
````

````markdown
## Section 1 decomposition addendum — Data Integration

### Section identity
- **Sprint:** 1
- **Section:** 1
- **Section Name:** Data Integration
- **Canonical Review File:** `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`

### Scope focus
This section covers the Section 1 data-integration portion of the canonical review script, including upstream live/source data ingestion and the creation of the initial integrated data objects handed forward to later Sprint 1 sections.

### Reviewer should trace

#### 1. Company resolution / company map lineage
- **Follow:** company-resolution logic → any review-mode filtering → resulting company map object/checkpoint
- **Why it matters:** confirms that the company universe is correct for the active run mode and that critical identifiers such as ticker and ISIN are populated

#### 2. Historical R&D / revenue pull
- **Follow:** historical R&D / revenue pull logic → resulting history object/sample output
- **Why it matters:** verifies that historical financial inputs are populated, structurally sound, and usable for downstream feature creation

#### 3. Patent data flow
- **Follow:** patent-source ingestion logic → merge/aggregation steps → resulting patent data object
- **Why it matters:** ensures patent-related inputs are classified and merged without obvious duplication or missingness problems

#### 4. Master snapshot construction
- **Follow:** snapshot creation logic → feature calculation → missing-value handling → final integrated snapshot object
- **Why it matters:** validates that data sources are joined correctly, deduplication works, and imputation/normalization behavior is visible before handoff to later sections

### Reviewer decomposition tasks
Please document in PR comments:

- what data enters Section 1
- what transformations occur before handoff to later sections
- what outputs leave Section 1
- what fields are materially transformed
- what dependencies or assumptions are hidden
- what should be validated before Section 2 review begins

Suggested reviewer tasks:

#### 1. Verify company resolution
- inspect the company mapping output
- confirm key identifiers such as ticker and ISIN are populated at acceptable coverage
- note obvious duplication or missing classification fields

#### 2. Inspect historical financial inputs
- inspect a small sample of historical R&D / revenue rows
- confirm fields are non-negative where expected
- note missingness, suspicious zeros, or obvious outliers

#### 3. Validate patent data flow
- inspect whether known AI-relevant firms show plausible nonzero AI-patent activity where expected
- note whether patent-source labeling/merge behavior appears reasonable

#### 4. Review imputation / normalization behavior
- inspect imputation flags or equivalent missing-data handling outputs
- confirm that imputed values appear plausible and non-pathological

### Key supporting files / modules to inspect

#### `R/02_ingest/01_company_resolution.r`
- **Purpose:** company/entity/ticker resolution logic
- **Why inspect it:** correct mapping of identifiers affects all downstream joins

#### `R/02_ingest/02_pull_rd_history.r`
- **Purpose:** historical R&D / revenue pull logic
- **Why inspect it:** provides financial inputs used later in feature construction

#### `R/02_ingest/03_pull_holdings.r`

#### `R/03_transform/01_create_snapshot.r`
- **Purpose:** snapshot construction logic
- **Why inspect it:** this is the main integration point where source alignment and duplication risk appear

#### Related transform / missing-value handling scripts used by the review file
- **Purpose:** derived-field and imputation logic
- **Why inspect them:** reviewers should confirm what transformations occur before Section 2 begins

### Important outputs to inspect
- company mapping object (for example, `company_map` or equivalent)
- historical R&D / revenue object (for example, `rd_history` or equivalent)
- integrated snapshot object (for example, `master_snapshot` or equivalent)

### Important fields / columns to trace
- `Ticker` / `ticker`
- `ISIN`
- country / industry classification fields
- `rd_expense`
- `revenue`
- patent-related counts/flags

#### 1. Source ingestion scripts

  - whether any warnings/errors appear during script loading

#### 2. Company resolution
- **Requirement / question:** company-resolution output should contain expected identifier fields and avoid obvious duplicate ticker rows

  - sample rows from the company mapping output
  - missingness in key identifier fields such as ticker and ISIN
  - whether obvious duplicates exist for primary entity/ticker keys
  - whether classification fields such as country/industry appear populated

#### 3. Historical R&D / revenue pull
- **Requirement / question:** historical financial inputs should be structurally usable and reasonably populated
- **Reviewer should inspect:**
  - sample rows for a few representative tickers
  - time coverage and continuity where visible
  - obvious missingness, suspicious zeros, or implausible values
  - whether downstream feature construction appears feasible from the data returned

#### 4. Holdings-related upstream ingestion
````

````markdown
### Key supporting files / modules to inspect
````

---

# Difference between full version and Option 3

## Full version
Best when:
- reviewers need more guidance
- code is complex
- team is new to the repo
- you want stronger decomposition discipline

### Pros
- very thorough
- more structured
- easier to use as a worksheet

### Cons
- long
- heavier to read in GitHub
- may overwhelm some reviewers

---

## Option 3
Best when:
- you want a lighter PR comment
- reviewers are already somewhat technical
- you want less scrolling
- you want something that feels more like a quick review guide

### Pros
- shorter
- cleaner
- easier to skim

### Cons
- less detailed
- fewer explicit review prompts
- may leave some reviewers needing more support

---

# My recommendation
For your team, I’d use:

# Section 1 decomposition addendum — Option 2.5

````markdown
## Section 1 decomposition addendum — Data Integration

### Scope focus
Review the Section 1 data-integration portion of `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`, including upstream source ingestion and creation of the initial integrated data objects handed forward to later sections.

### Reviewer should trace

#### 1. Company resolution / company map lineage
- Follow company-resolution logic through any review-mode filtering into the final company map object.
- Confirm that key identifiers such as ticker and ISIN are populated and usable.

#### 2. Historical R&D / revenue pull
- Follow historical financial pull logic into the resulting history object/sample output.
- Confirm that the data is structurally usable for downstream feature generation.

#### 3. Patent data flow
- Follow patent-source ingestion through merge/aggregation into final patent-related outputs.
- Check for obvious duplication, missingness, or source-labeling issues.

#### 4. Master snapshot construction
- Follow snapshot creation, feature calculation, and missing-value handling into the final integrated snapshot.
- Confirm joins, deduplication, and imputation behavior before handoff to later sections.

### Key supporting files / modules to inspect
- **`R/02_ingest/01_company_resolution.r`**
  - company/entity/ticker resolution logic
- **`R/02_ingest/02_pull_rd_history.r`**
  - historical R&D / revenue pull logic
- **`R/02_ingest/03_pull_holdings.r`**
  - holdings-related upstream ingestion logic
- **`R/02_ingest/04_pull_daily_ratios.r`**
  - daily ratio / volatility-related upstream inputs
- **`R/02_ingest/05_patentsview.r`** and related patent-source scripts
  - patent-related upstream inputs
- **`R/03_transform/01_create_snapshot.r`**
  - snapshot construction logic
- **Related transform / missing-value handling scripts**
  - derived-field and imputation logic used before Section 2

### Important outputs to inspect
- company mapping object
- historical R&D / revenue object
- integrated snapshot object

### Important fields / columns to trace
- `Ticker` / `ticker`
- `ISIN`
- country / industry classification fields
- `rd_expense`
- `revenue`
- patent-related counts/flags
- imputation flags or equivalent missing-value indicators

### Line-item review requirements

#### Source ingestion scripts
- confirm required scripts exist and load successfully
- note missing-file, path, warning, or sourcing issues

#### Company resolution
- confirm expected identifier fields are present
- inspect missingness and obvious duplicate ticker/entity rows
- confirm classification fields are populated where expected

#### Historical R&D / revenue pull
- inspect sample rows for structural usability
- note missingness, suspicious zeros, or implausible values

#### Holdings-related upstream ingestion
- confirm upstream holdings ingestion creates usable handoff inputs for Section 2
- note schema or identifier concerns visible at this stage

#### Patent data ingestion / merge
- inspect whether patent-related outputs are created and merged plausibly
- note duplication, missingness, or source-labeling concerns

#### Snapshot construction and missing-value handling
- confirm source inputs are joined coherently
- inspect duplicate behavior, missingness patterns, and imputation visibility
- confirm the integrated snapshot is structurally ready for Section 2

### Reviewer output
Please document in PR comments:
- what data enters Section 1
- what transformations occur
- what outputs leave Section 1
- what fields are materially transformed
- what dependencies or assumptions are hidden
- what should be validated before Section 2 review

### Reviewer validation support note
If reviewer-support code has been implemented, also verify:
- Section 1 checkpoint files are written successfully
- validation summaries exist and are readable
- review-mode execution stops after Section 1 when configured

If not, please note what intermediate objects remain available only in memory and what additional support would improve validation.
````

# Why Option 2.5 is probably the sweet spot

## Compared to full version
It removes:
- the very long line-item expansions
- some repeated explanations
- extra wording around reviewer behavior

But it keeps:
- trace paths
- supporting files
- outputs
- fields
- line-item requirements
- validation support note

## Compared to Option 3
It adds back:
- more structure
- line-item review requirements
- slightly better reviewer discipline
- more actionable decomposition guidance

---

# My recommendation
If you want something that is:
- serious
- organized
- not too long
- still strong enough for novice reviewers

then **Option 2.5 is probably the best version to use**.

## Suggested use
- **Section 1:** Option 2.5
- **Section 2:** Option 2.5
- **Section 3:** Option 2.5

That gives all three Sprint 1 PRs a consistent format without overwhelming the team.

If you want, I can now draft the **Section 2 addendum in Option 2.5 format**.

## @sganesan_dumac

Lets go with Option 1 for Section 1, and then Option 2.5 for Sections 2 & 3. Thoughts?

## Copilot

Yes — **that’s a very good choice**.

## My view
That is probably the **best practical mix** for your situation.

### Why it works
- **Section 1** is the first reviewer experience and the pilot section.
  - It needs the most guidance.
  - It introduces the reviewer to the whole decomposition method.
  - It sets the tone for the rest of Sprint 1.
- **Sections 2 and 3** can then be slightly lighter.
  - reviewers will already understand the workflow
  - you avoid overloading later PRs
  - you keep consistency without excessive length

So:

- **Section 1 = Option 1**
  - full, highly guided, detailed
- **Section 2 = Option 2.5**
  - medium detail
- **Section 3 = Option 2.5**
  - medium detail

I think that is the right balance of:
- rigor
- usability
- reviewer attention span
- implementation effort

---

# Why this is stronger than using the same format for all three

## If all three were full
That might be too much scrolling and repetition.

## If all three were short
Section 1 would probably be under-supported, and that is the section where you most need reviewer orientation.

## Mixed approach
This lets Section 1 act as:
- the “teaching PR”
- the most guided decomposition example

Then Sections 2 and 3 become:
- more efficient follow-ons

That is a smart reviewer-experience design.

---

# Recommended final approach

## Section 1 PR
Use:
- short PR body
- reviewer instructions comment
- **full Option 1 decomposition addendum**
- shared N=25 comment

## Section 2 PR
Use:
- short PR body
- reviewer instructions comment
- **Option 2.5 decomposition addendum**
- shared N=25 comment

## Section 3 PR
Use:
- short PR body
- reviewer instructions comment
- **Option 2.5 decomposition addendum**
- shared N=25 comment

- Reviewer output

The only difference should be:
- Section 1 has more detail under each heading
- Sections 2 and 3 are more concise

That way the reviewers learn one pattern and can reuse it.

# Section 2 decomposition addendum — Option 2.5

````markdown
## Section 2 decomposition addendum — Load Holdings Data

### Scope focus
Review the Section 2 holdings-loading portion of `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`, including how holdings-related data is introduced, normalized, matched, and handed forward to later pipeline logic.

### Reviewer should trace

#### 1. Holdings input ingestion
- Follow the holdings-loading logic from source input through the initial holdings object.
- Confirm that expected holdings-related identifier and exposure fields are present.

#### 2. Holdings normalization / cleaning
- Follow how holdings records are standardized, filtered, or reshaped before matching.
- Confirm that key fields used for downstream matching remain usable.

#### 3. Holdings-to-company matching
- Follow how holdings records are matched to company/entity identifiers.
- Confirm whether ISIN-, ticker-, or name-based matching behaves plausibly and whether unmatched cases are visible.

#### 4. Holdings handoff to downstream logic
- Follow the final holdings-related object created for later sections.
- Confirm that the output is structurally ready for Section 3 and later downstream use.

### Key supporting files / modules to inspect
- **`R/02_ingest/03_pull_holdings.r`**
  - holdings-related ingestion logic
- **Holdings-processing logic inside `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`**
  - matching, cleanup, and handoff behavior used in the canonical review file
- **Company mapping object from Section 1**
  - used to match holdings to known entities
- **Related helper logic used for identifier matching**
  - inspect any functions or code paths that depend on ISIN, ticker, or security-name matching

### Important outputs to inspect
- raw holdings object or equivalent upstream holdings data structure
- normalized holdings/matching object (for example, `holdings_lookup` or equivalent)
- any portfolio membership flags or holdings-derived fields handed forward downstream

### Important fields / columns to trace
- `ISIN`
- ticker / security identifier fields
- company/entity matching keys
- holdings name / security name fields
- exposure / weight / position-related fields
- portfolio inclusion or holdings-match flags

### Line-item review requirements

#### Holdings ingestion
- confirm holdings data loads successfully from the expected source
- note path, schema, or access issues that would block reviewer execution

#### Holdings normalization / cleanup
- inspect whether key identifier and exposure fields remain intact after cleaning
- note transformations that may silently alter matching behavior

#### Holdings matching logic
- confirm the matching logic is understandable and reviewable
- inspect whether unmatched or ambiguously matched holdings are surfaced clearly
- note any hidden ordering assumptions or fallback logic

#### Holdings output structure
- confirm the final holdings-related output is structurally usable downstream
- inspect whether later sections would receive the fields they need

#### Section boundary discipline
- keep the review focused on holdings loading, matching, and handoff
- note downstream scoring or portfolio-construction logic only if it is required to understand the Section 2 output

### Reviewer output
Please document in PR comments:
- what holdings data enters Section 2
- how the data is cleaned, transformed, or matched
- what outputs leave Section 2
- what fields are materially transformed
- what hidden dependencies or ordering assumptions exist
- what should be validated before Section 3 or downstream use

### Reviewer validation support note
If reviewer-support code has been implemented, also verify:
- Section 2 checkpoint files are written successfully
- holdings validation summaries exist and are readable
- review-mode execution stops after Section 2 when configured

If not, please note what intermediate holdings objects remain available only in memory and what additional support would improve reviewer validation.
````

# New implementation-ready pseudo-diff plan
## Target file
- `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`

## Supporting new files
- `R/01_utils/review_support.R`
- `run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

---

## A. Add a reviewer-support helper file

### New file
`R/01_utils/review_support.R`

### Purpose
Provide a reusable review-support layer without cluttering core pipeline logic.

### Functions to add
- `is_review_mode()`
- `get_stop_after_section()`
- `setup_review_output_dir()`
- `review_log()`
- `save_review_checkpoint()`
- `write_validation_summary()`
- `stop_if_review_section_complete()`

### Pseudo-diff
```r
# R/01_utils/review_support.R

is_review_mode <- function() {
  identical(tolower(Sys.getenv("RUN_REVIEW_MODE", "false")), "true")
}

get_stop_after_section <- function() {
  Sys.getenv("STOP_AFTER_SECTION", "")
}

setup_review_output_dir <- function() {
  run_timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
  base_dir <- here::here("outputs", "section_checkpoints", paste0("run_", run_timestamp))
  dir.create(base_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  return(base_dir)
}

review_log <- function(...) {
  cat("[REVIEW MODE]", ..., "\n")
}

save_review_checkpoint <- function(obj, path, format = c("csv", "rds")) {
  format <- match.arg(format)
  dir.create(dirname(path), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

  if (format == "csv") {
    write.csv(obj, path, row.names = FALSE)
  } else if (format == "rds") {
    saveRDS(obj, path)
  }
}

write_validation_summary <- function(obj,
                                     file_path,
                                     obj_name,
                                     critical_cols = NULL,
                                     unique_key = NULL) {
  lines <- c(
    paste("Validation summary for:", obj_name),
    paste("Timestamp:", Sys.time())
  )

  if (!is.null(dim(obj))) {
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Rows:", nrow(obj)))
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Columns:", paste(colnames(obj), collapse = ", ")))
  } else {
    lines <- c(lines, "Object is not tabular")
  }

  if (!is.null(critical_cols) && !is.null(colnames(obj))) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(critical_cols, colnames(obj))
    if (length(missing_cols) == 0) {
      lines <- c(lines, "Missing critical columns: none")
    } else {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("Missing critical columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
    }

    for (col in intersect(critical_cols, colnames(obj))) {
      lines <- c(lines, paste("NA count for", col, ":", sum(is.na(obj[[col]]))))
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(unique_key) && !is.null(colnames(obj)) && unique_key %in% colnames(obj)) {
    lines <- c(lines, paste("Duplicate", unique_key, "count:", sum(duplicated(obj[[unique_key]]))))
  }

  writeLines(lines, con = file_path)
}

stop_if_review_section_complete <- function(section_num) {
  stop_after <- get_stop_after_section()

  if (is_review_mode() &&
      nzchar(stop_after) &&
      identical(as.character(section_num), stop_after)) {
    review_log("Stopping after section", section_num)
    stop(
      structure(
        list(message = paste("REVIEW_STOP_SECTION", section_num)),
        class = c("review_stop", "condition")
      )
    )
  }
}
```

---

## B. Source the helper early in the true-review file

### Add near the top of `DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r`
Place after packages are loaded and before Section 1 logic begins.

### Pseudo-diff
```r
library(here)

source(here("R", "01_utils", "review_support.R"))

review_mode <- is_review_mode()
review_output_dir <- NULL

if (review_mode) {
  review_output_dir <- setup_review_output_dir()
  review_log("Reviewer output directory:", review_output_dir)
}

review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if (review_mode && exists("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- get("review_sample_tickers", envir = .GlobalEnv)
}
```

---

## C. Add review-mode N=25 filtering support
This is optional if the main script already supports a smaller sample, but likely needed.

### After `company_map` or equivalent Section 1 universe object is created
### Pseudo-diff
```r
if (review_mode && !is.null(review_sample_tickers)) {
  ticker_col <- NULL
  if ("Ticker" %in% colnames(company_map)) {
    ticker_col <- "Ticker"
  } else if ("ticker" %in% colnames(company_map)) {
    ticker_col <- "ticker"
  }

  if (!is.null(ticker_col)) {
    company_map <- company_map[company_map[[ticker_col]] %in% review_sample_tickers, ]
    review_log("Filtered company_map to review sample. Rows:", nrow(company_map))
  } else {
    review_log("No ticker column found in company_map; review sample filter not applied.")
  }
}
```

---

## D. Add Section 1 checkpoint saves

### After `company_map` is created
```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")

  save_review_checkpoint(
    company_map,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_company_map.csv"),
    "csv"
  )

  ticker_key <- if ("Ticker" %in% colnames(company_map)) "Ticker" else if ("ticker" %in% colnames(company_map)) "ticker" else NULL

  write_validation_summary(
    company_map,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_company_map_validation.txt"),
    "company_map",
    critical_cols = intersect(c("Ticker", "ticker", "ISIN"), colnames(company_map)),
    unique_key = ticker_key
  )
}
```

### After `rd_history` or equivalent historical financial object is created

```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")
  rd_sample <- utils::head(rd_history, 100)

  save_review_checkpoint(
    rd_sample,

    critical_cols = intersect(c("Ticker", "ticker", "revenue", "rd_expense"), colnames(rd_history)),
    unique_key = NULL

}
```

### After `master_snapshot` or equivalent integrated object is created
```r
if (review_mode) {
  section1_dir <- file.path(review_output_dir, "section1")

  save_review_checkpoint(
    master_snapshot,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot.rds"),
    "rds"
  )

  save_review_checkpoint(
    master_snapshot,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot.csv"),
    "csv"
  )

  ticker_key <- if ("Ticker" %in% colnames(master_snapshot)) "Ticker" else if ("ticker" %in% colnames(master_snapshot)) "ticker" else NULL

  write_validation_summary(
    master_snapshot,
    file.path(section1_dir, "section1_master_snapshot_validation.txt"),
    "master_snapshot",
    critical_cols = intersect(c("Ticker", "ticker"), colnames(master_snapshot)),
    unique_key = ticker_key
  )
}

stop_if_review_section_complete(1)
```

## G. Build the wrapper script

### New file
`run_review_sprint1_sections_1_3.R`

### Purpose
Give reviewers and you a single controlled way to run Sections 1–3 on the N=25 sample.

### Pseudo-diff
```r
library(here)
library(utils)

Sys.setenv(RUN_REVIEW_MODE = "true")
Sys.setenv(STOP_AFTER_SECTION = "3")

n25_path <- here::here("data", "00_reference", "n25_representative_sample.csv")
n25_df <- read.csv(n25_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

review_sample_tickers <- NULL
if ("ticker" %in% names(n25_df)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- unique(n25_df$ticker)
} else if ("Ticker" %in% names(n25_df)) {
  review_sample_tickers <- unique(n25_df$Ticker)
} else {
  stop("N=25 sample file must contain a 'ticker' or 'Ticker' column.")
}

assign("review_sample_tickers", review_sample_tickers, envir = .GlobalEnv)

tryCatch(
  {
    source(here::here("DAII_3.5_TRUE_For_Review_06_01_2026.r"))
    cat("Sprint 1 review run completed.\n")
  },
  review_stop = function(e) {
    message("Review-mode stop reached as expected: ", e$message)
  }
)
```

---

# Implementation notes
This new pseudo-diff assumes the corrected true-review file is structurally sane enough that we no longer need to:
- guard a premature top Section 2 execution block
- work around `company_map` being referenced before creation
- handle duplicate early/later holdings blocks specially

If, after inspection, the corrected file still has any of those artifacts, we can add back a small guard — but the plan above is the clean baseline.

````markdown
## Reviewer instructions

Please review only the in-scope script region identified in the PR body.

This canonical review script contains multiple downstream pipeline sections. Approval for this PR applies only to the scoped region listed in the PR body and does not imply approval of the full integrated pipeline script.

Please provide feedback in:
- line comments under **Files changed** for code-specific issues
- PR conversation comments for scope, validation, decomposition findings, and overall review notes

Please identify:
- logic issues
- dependency/order concerns
- hidden reliance on global state
- validation gaps
- missing tests or documentation

Please also document:
- what data enters this section
- what transformations occur
- what outputs leave this section
- what fields are materially transformed
- what dependencies or assumptions are hidden

Severity and final governance disposition remain in Jira.
````
